人们开玩笑说他们经常使用什么IDE，比如Word、MS记事本，有时甚至是Eclipse，然后经常是MS Paint。人们拿MS Paint开玩笑是因为它甚至不是一个文本编辑器，人们拿它开玩笑是因为它和IDE没有一个共同之处。这个应用程序提升了MS Paint的性能，让MS Paint只需单击几下按钮就可以突出显示、编译和执行代码，而且只需单击来自MS Paint的文本。现在它比Word、记事本，显然还有Eclipse要实用得多。
MS Paint IDE在过去的一年里增长了惊人的数量，我计划在接下来的一年里增长得更多。目前，以下是MS Paint IDE中提供的一些功能：
右键单击上下文菜单以打开和编辑MS Paint实例中的任何文本文件。
以下是安装和使用IDE的视频(仅供使用，请跳至2：18)。
MS Paint IDE是一个与MS Paint一起运行的应用程序，它可以读取您从MS Paint生成的图像。然后，它使用自定义OCR来读取、解析、突出显示和执行文本。所有输出文件和日志都创建为图像，以显示程序输出和编译/解释输出。
每个功能、按钮、使用说明以及您想要的任何东西都在Wiki上，如果您对试用IDE感兴趣，这是最好的起点。Https://wiki.ms-paint-i.de/。
贡献者总是受欢迎的，对于在本地设置存储库，有一个贯穿整个过程的维基页面：https://wiki.ms-paint-i.de/developing.。如果您只是有bug或功能请求，请使用相应的模板在GitHub上提出问题。
通过维基可以看到很多截图，但是下面有一些展示IDE的截图，所以你不需要深入研究它。
我现在主要做这个项目已经一年多了，虽然我没有从中赚钱，但我将非常感谢任何帮助支付域名和网站VPS费用的捐款。如果你捐款并想在这里被提及，只要把它放在捐款信息中就行了。欢迎通过PayPal或Open Collective捐款。

note_en:
People joke about what IDE they use often, things like Word, MS Notepad, sometimes even  Eclipse, and then often times MS Paint. People joke about MS Paint because it's not even a text editor, people joke about it because it doesn't have one feature in common with IDEs. Well, this application gives MS Paint a boost, and lets MS Paint highlight, compile, and execute code, with just a few clicks of a button, and only text coming from MS Paint. It is now much more practical than things like Word, Notepad, and obviously  Eclipse.
  MS Paint IDE has grown an insane amount over the past year, and I plan for it to grow even more in the following year. Currently, these are some of the features present in MS Paint IDE:
 Right-click context menu to open and edit ANY text file in an MS Paint instance
 The following is a video of the IDE being installed and used (For just usage, skip to  2:18)
   MS Paint IDE is an application that runs alongside MS Paint that reads its images you make from MS Paint. It then uses a custom  OCR to read, parse, highlight, and execute the text. All output files and logs are created as images, to show you the program output, and compilation/interpretation output.
  Every feature, button, usage instructions, and whatever you want is on the wiki, which is the best place to start if you're interested in trying out the IDE.  https://wiki.ms-paint-i.de/
  Contributors are always welcome, and for setting up the repository locally, there's a wiki page walking through the whole process:  https://wiki.ms-paint-i.de/developing. If you simply have a bug or feature request, please make a issue on GitHub using the corresponding template.
    Many screenshots are available through  the wiki, but some showing off the IDE are below so you don't need to dig through it.
       I've been doing mainly this project for over a year now, and even though I'm not making money off of it, I would be extremely appreciative for any contributions to help pay for the domain and the website's VPS. If you donate and want to be mentioned here, just put it in the donation message. Feel free to donate via  PayPal or  Open Collective.

note: 微卷曲对于较小的系统是卷曲的。在默认情况下，微卷曲禁用的所有功能都可以逐个启用，但可能会增加内存占用和内存需求。为了缩小微卷曲的占用空间而进行的尽可能多的机制被反向移植并合并到真正的卷曲中。Micro-curl的目标是为HTTPS提供存储大小在100K以内(在32位系统上)的获取能力，其中包括wolfSSL。微卷曲本身需要大约20K的动态内存(但是使用更多可能会加快传输速度)。Tiny-Curl是在GPLv3下授权的。请注意，这与麻省理工学院许可的常规卷发不同。
note_en: tiny-curl is curl for smaller systems.   All features that tiny-curl disables by default are possible to enable, piecemeal, but might increase footprint and memory requirements. As many as possible of the mechanisms that are done to shrink the footprint for tiny-curl are backported and merged into real curl.  The goal of tiny-curl is to offer HTTPS GET powers within  100K of storage size (on a 32 bit system), and that includes wolfSSL. tiny-curl itself requires about  20K of dynamic memory (but using more may speed up transfers).  tiny-curl is licensed under GPLv3. Note that this is different than the regular curl, which is MIT licensed.
title: 微卷曲7.72.0发布

note:
亚马逊生鲜(Amazon Fresh)负责人詹姆斯·赫尔布林(James Helering)在一篇博客文章中指出，这家商店将从上午7点开始，最初只接受邀请，并将向当地人发送电子邮件。至晚上10点。PT.。随着时间的推移，它将更新更多的开放时间和容量。
你可能在想，亚马逊已经有Whole Foods和规模较小的Amazon Go商店了吗？这里的想法是针对不同的客户从头开始建立一种新的杂货店体验。事实上，这是所有消费包装商品的必经之路：拥有针对所有人群的各种各样的品牌，你就会拥有自己的空间。
这也是亚马逊更广泛地与沃尔玛(Walmart)等公司更公平竞争的战术的重要组成部分，沃尔玛在美国的实体(因此，也就是所有)购物领域占据主导地位。根据Statista的这张图表，沃尔玛估计在美国食品杂货销售中占有约26%的市场份额，而这个市场仍然相当分散。数据显示亚马逊的Whole Foods份额仅为1.6%，尽管亚马逊自己估计这一比例更接近4%左右，包括其包括线上在内的其他渠道。尽管如此，它仍然相当小。
全食超市(Whole Foods)主要专注于有机和健康食品(由于在那里购物的费用很高，因此理所当然地获得了“全薪支付”(Whole Paycheck)的绰号)，Go规模较小，以其无人、全自动化、人工智能和相机的方式迎合早期采用者，而亚马逊生鲜(Amazon Fresh)除了亚马逊自己蓬勃发展的自有品牌系列外，还将引入一系列公认的主流大品牌，以及许多预先准备好的商品。
这并不是说它也不会非常依赖科技。这家商店将有一项名为亚马逊Dash Cart的新功能，这样人们就可以在进入商店之前建立商品清单，然后用它来选择和支付物品，以缩短打电话和用人工收银员付款的等待时间。商店周围将设置Echo Show设备，为人们提供哪里可以找到产品的建议。(但我认为它们不会是完全可操作的设备：即无法通过这些…更改商店中播放的音乐。至少现在还没有。)。
它还将向在该店购物的人提供免费送货服务，这基本上也将成为更广泛的亚马逊生鲜业务的某种仓库-到目前为止，亚马逊Fresh业务一直完全在网上运营(不久前，亚马逊已经开始提供免费送货服务，以加剧与现在市场上过多的其他杂货送货选择的竞争)。
通过从头开始建立整个商店，亚马逊也将更容易地将更多的技术融入体验中。
当涉及到实体店时，这种新型冠状病毒的到来、传播和持续存在以一种微妙的方式上演。根据你居住的地方，你在购物时需要遵守的规定可能不同，从要求戴口罩或限制进入或在商店内移动，到有其他限制的商店经营，在某些极端情况下根本不开门。
亚马逊表示，它计划根据全食超市(Whole Foods)的运作方式，对门店的运营方式采取自己的一套指导方针。员工将每天进行体温检测；员工和顾客都必须佩戴口罩；它将向需要的人提供免费的一次性口罩；商店的容量将被限制在最高50%。
对于那些一直关注该公司举措的人来说，洛杉矶这家门店的开业应该不会让他们大吃一惊：一段时间以来，该公司一直在慢慢挑选大型零售点，将其开发成新的仓库，包括在洛杉矶的地点，但包括招聘模式在内的其他一些信号让许多人猜测，更大的计划是将它们打造成零售业务。
据报道，该公司还一直在寻求在美国以外的其他市场开发实体店。多年来，英国一直有传言称，亚马逊一直在关注即将出售的零售地点，因为大型零售连锁店发现自己陷入了财务困境。与美国一样，英国的食品杂货业相当分散，有些混乱，由乐购(Tesco)主导。
具有讽刺意味的是，这种困境在一定程度上是人们转向在线购物的结果，比如在亚马逊(Amazon)这样的网站上购物。
note_en:
A blog post from James Helbling, the head of Amazon Fresh, notes that the store will open initially invitation-only, based emails it will be sending out to locals, from 7:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. PT. It will update more on opening hours and capacity over time  here.
 You might be thinking to yourself, but Amazon already has Whole Foods and smaller Amazon Go stores? The idea here will be to build a new grocery store experience from the ground up targeting a different customer. Indeed, this is par for the course with all consumer packaged goods plays: own a wide variety of brands targeting all demographics, and you will own the space.
  It’s also an essential part of the playbook for Amazon in its wider bid to compete more squarely against the likes of Walmart, which dominates the world of physical (and therefore, all) shopping in the US. Walmart was estimated to have about a 26% market share of grocery sales in the US, in what is still quite a fragmented market, according to  this graphic from Statista. The data puts Amazon’s Whole Foods share at just 1.6%, although Amazon itself  estimates that it is closer to about 4%,  including its other channels, including online . Still pretty small, nevertheless.
 While Whole Foods focuses mainly on organic and health foods (and has rightly earned the nickname “Whole Paycheck” because of how expensive a shopping trip can be there), and Go is smaller and about catering to early adopters with its no-human, all-automation, AI and camera approach, Amazon Fresh will bring in a bunch of recognised, mainstream big brands alongside Amazon’s own burgeoning own-label lines, along with a lot of pre-prepared items.
 That’s not to say it won’t also be very tech-heavy. The store will have a new feature called the Amazon Dash Cart so that people can build lists of items before going into the store, and then use that to select and pay for things to cut waiting time to ring up and pay with a human cashier. And there will be Echo Show devices set up around the store to give people advice on where to find products. (But I don’t think they will be fully operational devices: ie no ability to change the music playing in the store through these… not yet at least.)
 It will also offer free delivery to people who shop at the store, which essentially will also become a depot of sorts for the wider Amazon Fresh operation — which had been entirely online until now (and which had started to offer free delivery some time back to sharpen competition with the wide plethora of other grocery delivery options out in the market now).
 By building the whole store from the ground up, it will give Amazon to integrate more tech into the experience more easily, too.
 The arrival, spread, and persistent existence of the novel coronavirus has played out in a tricky way when it comes to physical stores. Depending on where you live, you will have different sets of regulations to comply with when shopping, which might range from requiring face masks or limiting entry or movement within stores, through to stores operating with other limitations and in some extreme cases not being opened at all.
 Amazon said it plans to take its  own set of guidelines into how the stores will be run, basing it on how Whole Foods has been working. Employees will have temperature checks daily; both workers and customers will have to wear face masks; it will offer free disposable masks to people who need one; and stores will be limited to a maximum capacity of 50%.
 The opening of this store in LA should not come as a huge surprise to those who have been following the company’s moves: it has been slowly picking up large retail locations to develop them into Fresh depots for a while now, including sites in LA, but a number of other signals including hiring patterns have led many to guess that the bigger plan was to build them into retail operations.
 The company has reportedly also been looking to develop physical grocery stores in other markets outside of the US as well. There have been rumors  swirling for years now in the UK — which like the US has a  pretty fragmented and somewhat tumultuous grocery industry, dominated in its case by Tesco — that Amazon has been eyeing up retail locations that have come up for sale as big retail chains have found themselves in financial dire straights.
 That predicament, ironically, has been partly the result of the shift to people shopping online, on sites like (you guessed it) Amazon.
title: 亚马逊在洛杉矶开设了第一家亚马逊生鲜实体店
title_en: Amazon opens its first Amazon Fresh physical grocery store, in LA

今天，司法部要求新冠肺炎的州长提供数据，这些州长发布的命令可能导致数以千计的养老院老人死亡。纽约州、新泽西州、宾夕法尼亚州和密歇根州要求养老院接纳新冠肺炎患者进入脆弱人群，通常没有进行足够的测试。
例如，2020年3月25日，纽约下令：“任何居民不得仅因新冠肺炎的确诊或疑似诊断而被拒绝重新入住或入住(养老院)，禁止(养老院)要求被确定为身体稳定的住院居民在入院或重新入院前对新冠肺炎进行检测。”
民权司助理总检察长埃里克·德雷班德(Eric Dreiband)表示：“保护包括养老院老年居民在内的一些社会最脆弱成员的权利，是我们国家最重要的义务之一。”“我们必须确保他们得到足够的照顾和尊严和尊重，不会把他们置于不必要的危险之中。”
根据疾病控制中心的数据，纽约是美国新冠肺炎死亡人数最多的州，有32,592名受害者，其中许多是老年人；按人口计算的死亡率为每百万人1,680人，居全国第二；新泽西州按人口计算的死亡率为每百万人1,733人--全国最高；相比之下，德克萨斯州按人口计算的死亡率为每百万人380人；德克萨斯州的死亡人数略高于11,000人，尽管其人口比纽约多50%，而且有记录的新冠肺炎病例要多得多-德克萨斯州为577,537例，纽约为430,885例。佛罗里达州的新冠肺炎死亡率为每百万人中有480人死亡；总死亡人数为10,325人，人口略高于纽约。
美国司法部民权司正在评估是否根据联邦《机构化人民权法案》(CRIPA)启动调查，该法案保护国营养老院人员的民事权利等，民权司寻求确定该州要求新冠肺炎患者入住养老院的命令是否对养老院居民的死亡负有责任。(编者按：美国司法部民权司正在评估是否应根据保护国营养老院人员民事权利的联邦《民权法案》启动调查，民权司寻求确定该州要求新冠肺炎患者进入养老院的命令是否对养老院居民的死亡负有责任。
2020年3月3日，司法部长宣布了司法部的全国养老院倡议，这是司法部在老年人正义倡议的领导下，与美国卫生与公众服务部结成强有力的合作伙伴关系，利用一切可用的工具追查为其居民提供不合标准护理的养老院的综合努力。正如2020年4月10日宣布的那样，该部门还在调查马萨诸塞州霍利奥克市的士兵之家，新冠肺炎在那里夺走了至少76名居民的生命。-https://www.justice.gov/opa/pr/federal-investigation-conditions-nursing-home-veterans-massachusetts-announced。
数据请求和士兵之家调查并不是对各州或任何其他个人或实体的过错或不当行为的指控，司法部尚未就这些问题得出任何结论。
note_en:
Today the Justice Department requested COVID-19 data from the governors of states that issued orders which may have resulted in the deaths of thousands of elderly nursing home residents. New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and Michigan required nursing homes to admit COVID-19 patients to their vulnerable populations, often without adequate testing.
 For example, on March 25, 2020, New York ordered: “No resident shall be denied re-admission or admission to [a nursing home] solely based on a confirmed or suspected diagnosis of COVID-19. [Nursing homes] are prohibited from requiring a hospitalized resident who is determined medically stable to be tested for COVID-19 prior to admission or readmission.”
 “Protecting the rights of some of society’s most vulnerable members, including elderly nursing home residents, is one of our country’s most important obligations,” said Assistant Attorney General for Civil Rights Division Eric Dreiband. “We must ensure they are adequately cared for with dignity and respect and not unnecessarily put at risk.”
 According to the Centers for Disease Control, New York has the highest number of COVID-19 deaths in the United States, with 32,592 victims, many of them elderly. New York’s death rate by population is the second highest in the country with 1,680 deaths per million people. New Jersey’s death rate by population is 1,733 deaths per million people – the highest in the nation. In contrast, Texas’s death rate by population is 380 deaths per million people; and Texas has just over 11,000 deaths, though its population is 50 percent larger than New York and has many more recorded cases of COVID-19 – 577,537 cases in Texas versus 430,885 cases in New York. Florida’s COVID-19 death rate is 480 deaths per million; with total deaths of 10,325 and a population slightly larger than New York.
 The Department of Justice’s Civil Rights Division is evaluating whether to initiate investigations under the federal “Civil Rights of Institutionalized Persons Act” (CRIPA), which protects the civil rights of persons in state-run nursing homes, among others. The Civil Rights Division seeks to determine if the state orders requiring admission of COVID-19 patients to nursing homes is responsible for the deaths of nursing home residents.
 On March 3, 2020, the Attorney General announced the Justice Department’s National Nursing Home Initiative. This is a comprehensive effort by the department, led by the Elder Justice Initiative and in strong partnership with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services that uses every available tool to pursue nursing homes that provide substandard care to their residents.  As announced on April 10, 2020, the department is also investigating the Soldiers’ Home in Holyoke, Massachusetts, where COVID-19 has taken the lives of at least 76 residents.   https://www.justice.gov/opa/pr/federal-investigation-conditions-nursing-home-veterans-massachusetts-announced
 The data requests and Soldiers’ Home investigation are not accusations of fault or wrongdoing by the states or any other individual or entity, and the department has not reached any conclusions about these matters.
title: 美国司法部终于启动新冠肺炎养老院死亡调查程序
title_en: DOJ Finally Starts Process of Investigating Nursing Home Deaths From COVID-19

你很有可能在过去的几个月里从亚马逊上买了东西(是的，我们都是伪君子，而且现在正在流行)。尝试在您的电子邮件中搜索其中一个订单。对我来说，那应该是“猫瓣”、“辣椒油”或“空气漂浮”。
没有运气吗？你并不孤单：亚马逊在几个月前就不再在订单确认和发货通知电子邮件中包含商品详细信息。他们现在只显示价格和订单日期。尽管亚马逊有很多缺点，但它拥有相当好的客户服务，这使得这种对用户怀有敌意的变化令人费解。当然，你仍然可以在亚马逊的网站上看到你的订单并下载CSV，但这比搜索你的电子邮件要麻烦得多；如果你是一名超级用户，你可以告别从亚马逊电子邮件自动生成待办事项。
是什么原因足以让亚马逊惹恼这么多用户呢？很简单：数据。
像爱迪生邮件(Edison Mail)和清洁福克斯(Cleanfox)这样的流行免费电子邮件客户端“刮掉”用户的电子邮件，并将匿名或化名的数据出售给第三方。有了足够的用户，他们就可以发现趋势并衡量品牌忠诚度-这对竞争对手来说是有价值的信息。通过去掉电子邮件中的商品细节，亚马逊告诉我们这对他们的伤害有多大。
话又说回来，亚马逊知道数据的价值。它被指责通过观察客户的使用情况来与自己的亚马逊网络服务(AWS)客户竞争：
批评人士认为，该公司作为平台的角色给了它不公平的优势。由于AWS拥有数以千计的客户，该公司对广泛的行业趋势有着神圣的视角，包括对哪些第三方工具最受欢迎的洞察力。一家开源云工具公司的一位高管私下告诉我，怀疑的是AWS正在关注“运行率”-每年在特定工具上花费的金额。当他们看到像Elastic这样的服务提供商开始产生可观的收入时，亚马逊就会将该工具的功能整合到自己的专有服务中。用沃伦的话说，这使得AWS既是球队老板又是裁判。
它还利用第三方供应商的私人数据推出了与之竞争的产品：
亚马逊员工访问了第三方供应商销售的一款畅销汽车后备箱管理器的文档和数据。这些信息包括总销售额，供应商向亚马逊支付了多少营销和运输费用，以及亚马逊每笔销售赚了多少钱。亚马逊的自有品牌部门后来推出了自己的汽车后备箱组织者。
对于亚马逊来说，扭亏为盈并不公平，但与除了使用AWS或在Amazon.com上销售之外别无选择的公司不同，它可以通过调整发送给客户的电子邮件来解决问题。就目前而言。
几年前，我与三星的全球战略小组(Global Strategy Group)就利用游戏化销售平视显示器和增强现实(AR)眼镜进行了长时间的通话。每一家大型科技公司都认为AR眼镜是智能手机之后的下一件大事，这是一个公开的秘密，所以他们的兴趣并不令人惊讶。我告诉他们，大多数游戏化都是糟糕和无效的，但看到三星四处寻找想法，似乎不确定人们会用AR眼镜做什么，这是一件令人着迷的事情。
AR的一些应用很明显，就像网页浏览和音乐在智能手机上很明显一样。我们都同意，看到方向在街道上空盘旋，而不是低头盯着手机；想象一下如何不用翻阅手册就能修理坏掉的咖啡机；如果你和我一样，在派对上看到人名在头顶飘扬，这将是有帮助的。但智能手机以大多数人没有预料到的方式更戏剧性地改变了世界，比如社交网络、出租车、摄影、游戏、零售和健身。
在我们拥有硬件之前想象AR的每一种用途都是愚蠢的事情，但AR眼镜有一种用途，很少有人在谈论，但它将改变世界：从我们看到的一切中抓取数据。
以亚马逊的电子邮件为例。如果客户开始佩戴AR眼镜，利用计算机视觉识别他们看到的一切-不仅是熟人的脸，而且是他们手机和笔记本电脑上的结账屏幕，那该怎么办？如果其他公司有可能窃取顾客与亚马逊的每一次互动，从他们的搜索词到他们浏览选项时的眼睛凝视，再到他们阅读的评论和他们访问的其他商店，那该怎么办？
亚马逊的情况也适用于所有其他小心翼翼地保护其“拥有的”数据的科技巨头。在短短几年的时间里，增强现实在那些拥有数据的人和那些想要数据的人之间开启了一场新的战争，这将是一场结束所有战争的战争。AR眼镜将以一种如此强大的方式收集和处理关于我们生活和整个世界的信息，以至于科技巨头们会觉得他们别无选择，只能严格锁定AR硬件-如此严格，以至于在十年后，他们对我们今天生活的相当大的控制将似乎是可笑的放任自流。
MyFitnessPal拥有数亿用户，是世界上最受欢迎的卡路里计数应用程序。它成功的原因之一是因为它拥有最完整的食物数据库。从制造商那里获取营养数据既昂贵又耗时，所以他们将其众包：
[创始人迈克·李(Mike Lee)]意识到，MyFitnessPal只有在他呼吁用户帮助[填充其食物数据库]的情况下才能真正扩大规模-换句话说，通过允许MyFitnessPal的用户输入和检查数据，将其众包。替代方案是从开放的食品营养数据库中获取数据，或者直接从食品制造商那里获得数据。然而，MyFitnessPal没有一个全面开放的条形码数据库可以使用，从食品制造商那里获取数据太麻烦了。此外，如果他们自己的用户输入了数据，那么MyFitnessPal将拥有这些数据。Facebook和Twitter等所谓的“社交”软件的一个决定性特征是，企业的价值几乎全部来源于其数据库中用户数据的数量和质量。MyFitnessPal也不例外，所以Mike Lee走众包路线是非常聪明的。用户输入MyFitnessPal食物数据库的所有内容都属于MyFitnessPal。
MyFitnessPal的经济护城河过去是，现在仍然是它的食物数据库。就像其他许多“web2.0”和早期的智能手机应用程序(Foursquare，Google Maps，Yelp，TripAdvisor，Waze等)一样。它利用和玩弄用户的劳动来积累数据，否则这些数据将难以收集。现在他们拥有了这些数据，竞争对手几乎不可能迎头赶上-当他们可以只用…来重建MyFitnessPal的食物数据库时，哪个社区会投入数百万小时来重建MyFitnessPal的食物数据库。使用MyFitnessPal吗？
增强现实可能会颠覆这一方程式。苹果的ARKit框架使单独开发人员能够创建应用程序，这些应用程序使用iPhone的摄像头立即识别和解析营养标签，大大减少了填充食品数据库所需的工作量。原则上，你可以很容易地将手机指向餐厅菜单或超市过道，或者为什么不指向亚马逊结账页面，或者任何其他类型的结构化视觉信息，这些信息都会得到类似的解析。
唯一的问题是，没有人真的想每隔五分钟拿出手机拍照一次。但是如果你的相机在你的眼镜上，那就完全不需要额外的努力了。应用程序将以比手工输入数据更快的速度和更高的精度，被动地全天吸纳这些数据。如果你的眼镜一直在看，你需要给你的亚马逊结账拍照。想象一下，带着一个价格比较AR应用程序漫步在超市的过道上，它会向你显示最划算的交易-同时默默地计算商店的库存。
让我们称它为掠夺世界吧。不难想到应用程序：将你读过的每一篇文章和每本书的每一个字存档，为你看过的电影和电视节目编目，保存你在商店或路人身上注意到的有趣的服装和衣服，根据你在社交媒体和现实世界中交谈的人绘制你的社交图，记录你上班的循环，以防你发生事故，等等。
当然，如果没有人想象在你的脸上戴着一个一直开着的相机，这一切都是泡汤的。但我怀疑，人们会发现AR指示、名牌、翻译、说明手册、社交媒体和游戏的诱惑实在太有趣了，太有用了，不能错过，所有这些都需要打开摄像头。
这就是说，一个永远在线的AR摄像头并不一定要侵犯任何人的隐私。软件可以理解和提取世界上的显著特征，而不需要直接访问摄像头源；处理可以在设备上完成，而不是在云中；收集的数据在共享之前可以正确地匿名。关键是，只要我们有严肃的法律和法规，AR眼镜和世界刮擦并不一定会导致监控或隐私启示录。
因此：擦拭世界的AR眼镜对那些业务和持续存在的公司构成了生存威胁，这些公司的业务和持续存在是基于精心收集、众包、购买，甚至在某些情况下被窃取的专有数据。这是很多公司，当然包括亚马逊。
2014年，我的团队在6岁时开始与谷歌的开发人员一起努力，让僵尸，快跑！为谷歌眼镜在英国的发布做好准备。我们之所以能做到这一点，是因为，正如我们现在所知，谷歌眼镜的硬件和操作系统令人深恶痛绝。虽然谷歌眼镜作为企业版仍然存在，但没有人相信这种特殊类型的AR眼镜会成为主流。
三年后，我试用了微软的Hololens，这是由微软首席交互设计师约书亚·沃尔顿(Joshua Walton)提供的演示程序提供的。HoloLens比谷歌眼镜好得多，拥有更宽的视野和更直观的手部跟踪输入，但仍落后于我们都喜欢的纤细AR眼镜几代人。
一年后，在XOXO音乐节的一个密室里，我戴上了Magic Leap One耳机。尽管该公司已经筹集了超过20亿美元，但它的速度很慢，容易过热，而且远未达到其嘶嘶卷轴的视觉质量。
这三款耳机都没有达到他们的炒作，但有两款会一直坚持下去，直到他们做对为止，其中一款--Magic Leap--几乎肯定不会。Magic Leap的几位数的数十亿美元，诚然是不正当的，不足以让尖端的传感器、显示屏和处理器以消费者的价格出售，也不足以写出一个全新的AR操作系统。这类游戏的赌注高达数百亿或数千亿美元，这个价格只有几个人能负担得起：微软、谷歌、苹果、亚马逊、Facebook、三星和华为，所有这些公司都将在AR上投资数十亿美元。
因此，今天主导我们生活的科技公司与最有可能制造下一代基本消费技术的公司是一样的。但我们一直都有大型科技公司，对吧？今天是苹果，昨天是戴尔，在此之前是IBM。不同的是，今天，这些公司不仅仅想卖给我们硬件。他们甚至不想只向我们出售他们自己的软件和内容-他们想从他们硬件上存在的所有软件和内容中分得一杯羹，并控制它来引导。
考虑到智能手机市场类似于自然垄断(没有人想要同时使用，更不用说携带两部智能手机了)，由于平台锁定的个人数据、之前购买的应用程序、iMessage等，切换成本很高，这一状况对苹果和谷歌来说一直是非常有利可图的，很难看到AR眼镜市场会有什么不同。
更重要的是，将生产我们的AR眼镜的巨头们不能允许他们的商业模式受到掠夺世界的威胁--或者更确切地说，是他们没有授权的掠夺世界。显然，谷歌会很高兴它的用户通过AR眼镜为他们众包更多的地图数据，但他们不会对人们使用他们的硬件和操作系统来构建一个与之竞争的系统印象深刻。同样，亚马逊也不会喜欢用户仅仅看书架就能组装出更好的Goodread。
他们当然不会喜欢Kindle用户仅仅通过阅读来撕毁他们的电子书：你可以复制保护Kindle文件，但你不能复制保护平板电脑屏幕上的像素。这就是刮世界的意义--你能看到的任何东西都可以用AR眼镜刮掉。
嗯，并不完全是：AR眼镜本身不能刮掉眼镜上显示的任何东西。因此，如果你卖给某人一本电子书，你真的不想让它的内容被刮掉，你只会在增强现实中展示它。同样的道理也适用于你不想刮掉的任何其他内容或数据：只需强迫人们在他们的AR眼镜中查看，而不是在手机或平板电脑屏幕上查看。
就像眼睛用的HDCP一样，公司可以拒绝在AR眼镜以外的显示器上显示任何有价值的数据或内容。
科技巨头们将吹捧AR眼镜令人敬畏的力量，同时也警告说，这种力量很容易被滥用。想象一下，安装一个恶意的AR应用程序，它会秘密记录你看到的一切！这足以把任何人吓进苹果(Apple)、谷歌(Google)或Facebook(Facebook)加持的应用程序的围墙花园，这是唯一大到足以对抗你选择的对手的公司，无论是有组织犯罪、俄罗斯黑客还是中国军方。不计其数的公司会希望使用AR眼镜来削减成本和提高生产率，但他们也会想要降低他们的数据被黑客窃取的风险。
科技巨头的反恐战争正在奏效：史诗大战苹果/谷歌的战斗已经看到了同样的不合历史的论点向巨头的支持者炫耀，即如果没有苹果和谷歌的监督，我们的手机将被病毒和恶意软件侵扰。这忽略了我们的过去，因为你不需要任何人的许可就可以编写软件或创建网站，这导致了个人电脑和互联网革命的蓬勃发展；更糟糕的是，它忽视了我们生活中的现实，在那里，任何人都可以在自己的Mac或PC上安装任何应用程序。
如果苹果和谷歌的“围墙花园”像苹果在Mac上那样，只将真正有害的软件拒之门外，那就是一回事了，但它们的控制延伸到了对有关工人剥削的严肃游戏的审查，以及对电子书出版和游戏流媒体等整个业务的有效禁止。
这些公司把手机当作家用电器，而不是通用电脑。为什么我们要期待苹果、谷歌、Facebook或亚马逊在AR眼镜方面有所不同呢？
Worldscraping有着令人难以置信的潜力。我们将能够在不需要亚马逊或谷歌许可的情况下提取我们自己的数据。我们可以做一个更好的食物数据库，更好的植物和野生动物目录，更好的世界地图，任何你能想象到的需要真实世界信息的东西，所有这些都需要更少的工作和更少的时间。
掠夺世界的最大输家将是现有公司和科技巨头。他们会想要继续为自己搜刮世界，他们会说这是因为只有他们才能被信任使用它。
但他们不是唯一能保证我们安全的人。有一些方法可以保护计算设备，而无需不负责任的看门人或专家用户。与苹果希望你相信的相反，我们每天都在使用软件，如果不是更安全的话，就像他们围墙花园里的任何东西一样安全。在我们迈向下一代计算设备时，我们需要支持这些第三方公司和开源社区，并向它们学习。
在这个新的世界里，科技巨头们有一席之地，如果他们愿意的话。帮助用户阻止恶意软件-就像苹果在Mac上所做的那样-不需要对软件分发进行令人窒息的控制。而且他们每年仍然可以从销售硬件和自己的服务中赚取数百亿美元。那不会太糟吧，不是吗？
谷歌眼镜是个笑话。增强现实并非如此，我们不能对它一笑置之或置之不理。这就是我们在智能手机和社交网络上所做的事情，现在我们在全球范围内发生了种族灭绝和反竞争的企业行为。
我们在2035年不能选择不戴AR眼镜，就像我们今天不能选择不拥有智能手机一样。数十亿人别无选择，只能使用智能手机完成基本任务，如教育、银行、通信和访问政府服务。在短短几年的时间里，AR眼镜也做了同样的事情，但速度更快，效果更好。他们还会做一些全新的、强大得令人难以置信的事情，比如刮世界。危险在于，那些将受到世界掠夺威胁的公司将控制使之成为可能的技术。
我们需要AR眼镜被当作功能强大的通用计算机来对待，这意味着所有用户和所有开发人员都可以开放访问。如果科技巨头不愿意自愿这么做，政府就需要强迫他们这么做。
另一种选择是不可想象的：在一个我们看到的东西和我们看到的地方都由科技巨头控制的世界里。
note_en:
Chances are you’ve bought something from Amazon in the last few months (yes, we are  all hypocrites, also there’s a pandemic on). Try searching your email for one of those orders. For me, that’d be “cat flap” or “chilli oil” or “airfloss”.
 No luck? You aren’t alone:  Amazon stopped including item details in order confirmation and shipping notification emails a few months ago. They just show the price and order date now. For all its faults, Amazon has pretty good customer service, which makes this user-hostile change baffling to understand. Sure, you can still see your orders on Amazon’s website and download a CSV, but it’s far more cumbersome than searching your email; and if you’re a power-user, you can say goodbye to  automatically generating to-do tasks from Amazon emails.
  What reason would be big enough for Amazon to annoy so many of their users? It’s simple: data.
 Popular free email clients like Edison Mail and Cleanfox “scrape” their users’ emails and  sell anonymised or pseudonymised data on to third parties. With enough users, they can detect trends and measure brand loyalty – valuable information for competitors. By stripping item details from its emails, Amazon tells us just how much this was hurting them.
 Then again, Amazon knows the value of data. It’s been accused of  competing with its own Amazon Web Services (AWS) customers by watching their usage:
 Critics argue that the company’s role as a platform gives it an unfair advantage. Because AWS has thousands of customers, the company has a godlike perspective of broad industry trends, including insight into which third-party tools are most popular. The suspicion, one executive of an open source cloud tool company told me off the record, is that AWS is watching “run rates” — the amount of money spent on a particular tool per year. When they see a service provider like Elastic start to generate serious revenue, Amazon incorporates the functionality of that tool into its own proprietary service. In the words of Warren, that makes AWS both a team owner and an umpire.
 And it’s also used the private data of third-party vendors to  launch competing products:
 Amazon employees accessed documents and data about a bestselling car-trunk organizer sold by a third-party vendor. The information included total sales, how much the vendor paid Amazon for marketing and shipping, and how much Amazon made on each sale. Amazon’s private-label arm later introduced its own car-trunk organizers.
 For Amazon, turnabout isn’t fair play, but unlike companies who have little choice but to use AWS or sell on Amazon.com, it can solve its problem by tweaking the emails it sends to customers. For now.
  A couple of years ago, I had a long call with Samsung’s Global Strategy Group about using gamification to sell heads-up displays and augmented reality (AR) glasses. It’s an open secret that every major tech company thinks AR glasses are the Next Big Thing after smartphones, so their interest wasn’t a surprise. I told them most gamification was awful and ineffective, but it was fascinating to see Samsung casting about for ideas, as if unsure what people would use AR glasses for.
  Some applications for AR are obvious in the same way that web browsing and music were obvious for smartphones. We can all agree it’d be helpful to see directions hovering over streets rather than peering down at a phone; to visualise how to repair a broken coffee machine without flipping through a manual; and if you’re like me, to see people’s names floating beside their heads at parties. But smartphones changed the world far more dramatically in ways that most people didn’t predict, like social networks, taxis, photography, games, retail, and fitness.
 It’s a fool’s errand to imagine every use of AR before we have the hardware in our hands, yet there’s one use of AR glasses that few are talking about but will be world-changing: scraping data from everything we see.
 Take the example of Amazon’s emails. What if customers start wearing AR glasses that use computer vision to recognise everything they see – not just acquaintances’ faces but the checkout screens on their phones and laptops? What if it’s possible for other companies to scrape customers’ every interaction with Amazon, from their search terms to their eye gaze as they scan through the options, to the reviews they read and the other stores they’re visiting?
 What’s true for Amazon is true for every other tech giant that jealously guards the data it “owns”. In just a few year’s time, augmented reality open a new war between those who hold data and those who want it, and it’ll be the war to end all wars. AR glasses will enable the collection and processing of information about our lives and the entire world in a way that’s so powerful, tech giants will feel they have no choice but to lock AR hardware down tight – so tight that in a decade’s time, their considerable control over our lives today will seem laughably laissez-faire.
    With hundreds of millions of users, MyFitnessPal is the most popular calorie counting app in the world. One reason why it succeeded is because it had the most complete food database. It was too expensive and time-consuming to get nutrition data from manufacturers, so  they crowdsourced it:
 [Founder Mike Lee] realized that MyFitnessPal would only truly scale if he called on his users to help [populate its food database] — in other words, crowdsourced it by enabling MyFitnessPal’s users to input and check data. The alternatives were to get the data from open food nutrition databases or directly pipe it from food manufacturers. However, there wasn’t a comprehensive open database of barcodes that MyFitnessPal could tap into and getting the data from food manufacturers was far too much work. Besides, if their own users entered the data then MyFitnessPal would own it. One of the defining characteristics of so-called “social” software — like Facebook and Twitter — is that the value of the business is almost all derived from the amount and quality of user data in its databases. MyFitnessPal is no exception, so Mike Lee was very smart to go the crowdsourcing route. Everything its users enter into MyFitnessPal’s food database belongs to MyFitnessPal.
 MyFitnessPal’s  economic moat was, and still is, its food database. Like so many other “web 2.0” and early smartphone apps ( Foursquare,  Google Maps,  Yelp,  TripAdvisor,  Waze, etc.) it exploited and gamified users’ labour to amass data that would otherwise be prohibitively difficult to collect. Now they own that data, it’s nearly impossible for competitors to catch up – what community will put in the million hours required to reconstruct MyFitnessPal’s food database when they could just… use MyFitnessPal?
  Augmented reality threatens to upend that equation. Apple’s  ARKit framework has made it possible for solo developers to create apps that use your iPhone’s camera to  instantly recognise and parse nutrition labels, vastly reducing the effort required to populate a food database. In principle, you could just as easily point your phone at a restaurant menu or a supermarket aisle or, why not, an Amazon checkout page, or any other kind of structured visual information, and it would be similarly parsed.
 The only problem is that no-one really wants to pull out their phone and take pictures every five minutes. But if your camera was on your glasses, it’d be no extra effort at all. Apps will passively hoover up this data throughout the day, at a greater speed and likely greater accuracy than inputting the data by hand. There’ll need to photo your Amazon checkout if your glasses are watching all the time. And imagine strolling down a supermarket aisle with a price-comparison AR app that shows you the best deals – while at the same time silently counting the store’s inventory.
  Let’s call it worldscraping. It’s not hard to think of applications: archiving every word of every article and book you read for searching later, cataloging the movies and TV shows you’ve watched, saving interesting outfits and clothes you notice in shops or on passers-by, mapping your social graph based on the people you talk to on social media and in the real world, recording your cycle into work in case you get into accidents, and on and on…
 Of course, if no-one fancies wearing an always-on camera on your face, this is all dead in the water. But I suspect people will find the lure of AR directions and nametags and translations and instruction manuals and social media and games far too entertaining and useful to pass up, and all of these things will require that camera to be on.
 That said, an always-on AR camera doesn’t  necessarily have to violate anyone’s privacy. Software can understand and extract salient features from the world without needing direct access to a camera feed; the  processing can be done on the device rather than in the cloud; and the collected data could be properly anonymised before being shared. The point is that AR glasses and worldscraping don’t inevitably lead to a surveillance or privacy apocalypse, providing we have serious legislation and regulation.
 So: worldscraping AR glasses pose an existential threat to companies whose business and continued existence is based on proprietary data that’s been painstaking collected, crowdsourced, purchased, or in some cases, stolen. And that’s a  lot of companies, including, of course, Amazon.
   In 2014, my team at Six to Start worked hard with Google’s developers to  get Zombies, Run! ready for the UK launch of Google Glass. We only just made it because, as we all know now, the Glass hardware and operating system was an utter abomination. While Google Glass lives on today as an  Enterprise Edition, no-one believes this particular breed of AR glasses will ever make it into the mainstream.
 Three years later, I tried out Microsoft’s Hololens, courtesy of a demo by Joshua Walton, its Principal Interaction Designer. Hololens was much better than Google Glass with a wider field of view and more intuitive hand-tracking input, but still generations behind the svelte AR glasses we’d all like.
 And a year after that, in a back room at the XOXO festival, I slipped on the Magic Leap One headset. Despite the company having raised over $2 billion, it was slow, prone to overheating, and nowhere near the visual quality in its sizzle reels.
 All three headsets failed to live up to their hype, but two will keep going until they get it right, and one of them – Magic Leap – almost certainly won’t. Magic Leap’s single-digit billions, admittedly misspent, wasn’t enough to make the cutting-edge sensors, displays, and processors available at a consumer price, or to write a whole new AR operating system. The ante for that kind of game runs into the tens or hundreds of billions, a price only a few can afford: Microsoft, Google, Apple, Amazon, Facebook, Samsung, and Huawei, all of whom will be investing billions into AR.
 So, the tech companies that dominate our lives today are the same that are most likely to manufacture the next generation of essential consumer technology. But we’ve always had big tech companies, right? Today it’s Apple, yesterday it was Dell, before that, IBM. The difference is that today, these companies don’t just want to sell us hardware. They don’t even want to just sell us their own software and content – they want to take a cut from  all software and content that exists on their hardware, and control it, to boot.
 Given that the smartphone market is akin to a  natural monopoly (no-one wants to use, let alone carry, two smartphones at once) with high switching costs due to platform-locked personal data, previously purchased apps, iMessage, etc., a state of affairs that has been very profitable for Apple and Google, it’s hard to see the AR glasses market from turning out any differently.
 What’s more, the giants who’ll make our AR glasses can’t permit the threat to their business models that worldscraping poses – or rather, worldscraping they don’t authorise. Clearly Google would be delighted for its users to crowdsource even more maps data for them via AR glasses, but they’d be less impressed with people using their hardware and OS to build a competing system. Similarly, Amazon won’t enjoy users assembling a better Goodreads by simply looking at their bookshelves.
 They certainly wouldn’t appreciate Kindle users ripping their ebooks just by, well, reading them: you can copy-protect a Kindle file, but you can’t copy-protect the pixels on a tablet screen. That’s the point of worldscraping – anything you can see can be scraped with AR glasses.
 Well, not  quite everything: AR glasses wouldn’t be able to scrape whatever’s displayed on the glasses themselves. So if you sell someone an ebook and you really didn’t want its contents to be scraped, you’d only display it in augmented reality. The same goes for any other content or data you don’t want scraped: just force people to view it in their AR glasses rather than on phone or tablet screens.
 Like  HDCP for your eyes, companies could refuse to display  any valuable data or content on displays other than AR glasses.
   Tech giants will tout the awesome power of AR glasses while also warning that such power is easily abused. Imagine installing a malicious AR app that secretly recorded everything you saw! It’s enough to scare anyone into a walled garden of apps blessed by Apple or Google or Facebook, the only companies big enough to stand up to your choice of adversary, whether that’s organised crime, Russian hackers, or the Chinese military. Countless companies will want to use AR glasses to cut costs and increase productivity, but they’ll also want to reduce the risk of their data being scraped by hackers.
 The tech giants’ War on Terror is working: the  Epic vs. Apple/Google fight has seen the same ahistorical arguments trotted out the giants’ supporters, that without Apple and Google’s oversight, our phones would be infested with viruses and malware. This ignores our past, which saw the PC and internet revolutions boom due to the fact that you didn’t need anyone’s permission to write software or create a website; and even worse, it ignores our lived reality, where anyone can install any app on their Mac or PC.
 It would be one thing if Apple and Google’s walled garden only kept out genuinely harmful software,  as Apple does on the Mac, but their control extends to  censorship of serious games about worker exploitation and the effective banning of entire businesses like ebook publishing and game streaming.
 These companies treat phones like appliances rather than general purpose computers. Why would we expect Apple or Google or Facebook or Amazon to do any different for AR glasses?
  Worldscraping has incredible potential. We’ll be able to extract  our own data without needing Amazon or Google’s permission. We can make a better food database, a better catalogue of plants and wildlife, a better map of the world, anything you can imagine that requires information about the real world, all with far less work and in far less time.
 The big losers from worldscraping will be incumbent companies and tech giants. They’ll want to keep worldscraping for themselves, and they’ll say it’s because only they can be trusted to use it.
 But they aren’t the only ones who can keep us safe. There are ways to secure computing devices without unaccountable gatekeepers or expert users. Contrary to what Apple would have you believe, we all use software every day that’s as safe as anything inside their walled garden, if not safer. We need to support and learn from those third-party companies and open source communities as we head into the next generation of computing devices.
 There’s a place for the tech giants in this new world, if they’ll take it. Helping users block malicious software – as Apple does on the Mac – doesn’t require a suffocating grip on software distribution. And they can still make tens of billions a year from selling hardware and their own services. That wouldn’t be so bad, would it?
  Google Glass was a joke. Augmented reality isn’t. We can’t just laugh at it or ignore it. That’s what we did with smartphones and social networks, and now we have genocides and anticompetitive corporate behaviour on a global scale.
 We won’t be able to opt out from wearing AR glasses in 2035 any more than we can opt out of owning a smartphones today. Billions have no choice but to use smartphones for basic tasks like education, banking, communication, and accessing government services. In just a few year’s time, AR glasses do the same, but faster and better. They’ll also do entirely new and incredibly powerful things, like worldscraping. The danger is that the companies who’ll be threatened by worldscraping are the ones who’ll control the technology that enables it.
 We need AR glasses to be treated as the incredibly powerful general purpose computers they will surely become, which means open access for all users and all developers. If tech giants won’t do that voluntarily, they need to be forced to do so by governments.
 The alternative is unthinkable: a world where what we see and where we see it is controlled by tech giants.
title: 数字视觉管理和亚马逊收据丢失之谜
title_en: Digital Sight Management, and the Mystery of the Missing Amazon Receipts

note:
前段时间，我写了一篇关于BBC微型修复和升级的帖子。这一次，我向你介绍一位英国广播公司的大师-Beebs哥哥🙂。
我前段时间买了这台BBC Master。它在架子上放了好一阵子，但最后，我终于开始着手做了。这一次，这又是我朋友温德尔的定制订单(干杯伙伴：)。
我还需要提到它是作为“非工作部件”出售的吗？：&gt；
嗯，事实证明，它的形状并不是戏剧性的，但需要刷新和升级。
案件的解决很容易。我只需要修理一个折断的螺柱，并用金属网和烙铁修补一些裂缝。
在打开电源之前，我想确保PSU工作正常。Beeb和Master中的PSU使用容易爆炸的盖子，在为其供电之前值得检查。我的部队证实了这一点。我不得不更换这些帽子，这是一件容易的工作。
温德尔让我试着让这台设备在美国和欧盟使用。这仅仅意味着PSU必须更灵活一些，应该可以接受110 VAC/60 Hz(美国)和230 VAC/50 Hz(EU)。
我非常幸运，因为这台BBC里面有一个三华田PSU，原来它支持两种工作模式，取决于跳线设置。
我只是简单地添加了一些电线和一个开关。我只需要在PSU的金属外壳上钻一个漂亮的洞，我就是黄金🙂。
接下来，我转到了PCB清洗。像往常一样，我把它倒进我的超声波清洗机里，以去除这些讨厌的白色晶体结构。我还把一个坏掉的扬声器换成了我在一个抽屉里找到的8欧姆扬声器🙂。
在来自一个很棒的stardot.org.uk论坛的人的帮助下，我设法找出了一个问题。事实证明，这是BBC Master的常见问题，我的设备只是缺了一个电池。看起来前车主为了防止泄漏和腐蚀把它移走了。
我从Mark of RetroClinic订购了一个3xAA的电池盒，效果非常好。它有一个在线焊接的二极管，以防止电池充电。缺点是电池必须不时地更换，但这仍然是一个比原来的解决方案好得多的解决方案。
当我在星点论坛上寻找我之前电池问题的解决方案时，我偶然发现了与BBC Micro Post-RPI Zero中相同的MOD作为合作专家。这一次它是一个内部模型，同样，它是由Retroclinic.com的Mark制造的。
他会把它连同一个电池托盘和一个很酷的装备一起寄给我，我稍后会谈到，😉。
我从Mark那里得到的最后一个附加组件是一台GoTek，它有一个OLED，所有需要的设备和一个已装满的USB闪存驱动器。
对于bbc Master来说，这是一个非常方便的解决方案，不出所料，它的工作方式就像一个迷人的🙂。
如果您想购买复古设备或硬件模块，请访问我们的商店-&gt；https://retrohax.net/shop/。
如果你想捐赠一台报废的电脑，那就给我发一封电子邮件。欢迎极端情况，🙂
note_en:
Some time ago, I wrote a post about  BBC Micro restoration and upgrades. This time, I present you a BBC Master – Beebs bigger brother 🙂
 I’ve bought this BBC Master some time ago. It was sitting on a shelf for quite a while but finally, I’ve managed to start working on it. This time again, it was a custom order for my friend Wendell (cheers mate:)
 Do I have to mention that it was sold as “For parts – non-working” ? :>
 Well, as it turned out, it wasn’t in a dramatic shape but required refreshing and upgrades.
          Case fixes were easy. I only had to fix a broken screw stud and fix some cracks with metal mesh and soldering iron.
     Before powering it on, I wanted to make sure that PSU is working properly. PSUs in Beeb and Master use caps that tend to explode and are worth checking before supplying power to it. My unit confirmed that. I had to replace these caps which was an easy job.
   Wendell asked me to try making this unit usable in the US and in the EU. This simply means that PSU has to be a bit more flexible and should accept 110VAC/60HZ (USA) and 230VAC/50Hz (EU)
 I was very lucky because this BBC had a San Hua Tien PSU inside and it turned out that it supports both working modes depending on a jumper setting.
    I’ve simply added some wires and a switch. I only had to make a nice hole in PSUs metal case and I was golden 🙂
            Next, I’ve moved to PCB cleaning. As usual, I’ve dumped it into my ultrasonic cleaner to get rid of these nasty white crystalline structures. I’ve also replaced a broken speaker with the 8 Ohm speaker that I’ve found in one of my drawers 🙂
         With the help of guys from an excellent  stardot.org.uk forum, I’ve managed to pinpoint a problem. It turned out that this is a common issue with BBC Master, and my unit was simply missing a battery. Looks like the previous owner removed it to prevent leakage and corrosion.
 I’ve ordered a 3xAA battery case from  Mark of RetroClinic and it worked like a charm. It has a diode soldered in-line to prevent batteries from charging. The downside is that batteries have to be replaced from time to time but still, this is a way better solution than the original one.
     While looking for a solution to my previous battery problem on  stardot forums, I’ve stumbled across the same mod as in  BBC Micro post – Rpi Zero as a co-pro. This time it is an internal mod and again, it is made by  Mark of retroclinic.com.
 He’d send it to me along with a battery tray and one more cool gear that I will talk about later 😉
           The last add-on that I’ve got from Mark was a GOTEK with an OLED, all needed gear and a filled USB flash drive for it.
 It is a very convenient solution for BBC Master and as expected, works like a charm 🙂
        If you want to get retro gear or hardware modules, please visit  our shop -> https://retrohax.net/shop/
  If you want to donate a dead computer then  drop me an email. Extreme cases are welcome 🙂
title: BBC大师服用类固醇

note:
自从1990年1月1日我不再有电子邮件地址以来，我一直是一个快乐的人。我大约从1975年就开始使用电子邮件了，在我看来，15年的电子邮件对我的一生来说已经足够了。
对于那些在生活中的角色是掌握最重要的事情的人来说，电子邮件是一件很棒的事情。但对我来说不是这样，我的角色是了解事情的底层。我所做的事情需要长时间的学习和不间断的专注。我试图详尽地学习计算机科学的某些领域；然后我试图将这些知识消化成一种形式，让那些没有时间学习的人可以接触到。
另一方面，当我写书时，我需要与世界各地成千上万的人交流。我也想回应那些读过这些书并有问题或评论的人。我的目标是以批处理的方式高效地进行这种交流-比如，每六个月一天。因此，如果您想就任何话题写信给我，请使用普通邮件，并将信件寄到以下地址：
我有一位很棒的秘书，她看着收到的邮件，把她知道我急切盼望看到的任何东西都分离出来，其他的东西都放入缓冲储存区，我会定期清空。
我的秘书还打印出所有发送到taocp@cs.stanford.edu或knuth-bug@cs.stanford.edu的非垃圾电子邮件，这样我就可以在有机会时用书面意见回复。如果我遇到这样一条写错了地址的信息-我的意思是，如果信息提出一个问题而不是报告一个错误-我尽量不生气-我过去只是把所有这样的纸扔进垃圾桶，但现在我把它们留作草稿纸，这样我就可以在空白的一面打印计算机编程艺术的测试材料。我不会回复不请自来的电子邮件，也不会回复发送给同事的令人烦恼的要求他们与我沟通的电子邮件。除了在书中报告错误之外，电子邮件是禁忌。
你也可以试着传真给我，号码是650-725-4671。但请注意，我最后一次查看收到的传真邮件，可能每12个月才查看一次，而不是6个月。
“我甚至没有电子邮件地址。我已经到了一个主要目的不是接收信息的年龄。&-翁贝托·艾柯，引用“纽约客”的话。
有时，当我相信收件人不会被我的请求打扰时，我确实会通过我的秘书发送关于我目前正在工作的项目的电子邮件。但我希望你能理解为什么我自己几乎总是不喜欢收到不请自来的电子邮件。感谢你在我试图完成计算机编程艺术(TAOCP)的过程中的耐心和合作，这是我从1962年开始的一项工作，我需要很多年才能完成。作为回报，我保证不会向你发送不受欢迎的电子邮件请求。
英语中新造的随机词通常用连字符拼写，但当这些词被广泛使用时，连字符就消失了。例如，人们习惯于写“非零”和“软件”，而不是写“非零”和“软件”，这一趋势在数百个其他单词中也出现了。因此，现在是每个人停止使用古老拼写“e-mail；”的时候了。想想看，如果你现在停止，你一生中会省下多少次击键！电子邮件这种形式在英国已经流行了好几年了，所以我很惊讶地看到美国人在这方面过于保守。(当然，‘电子邮件’在法国、德国和荷兰是一个熟悉的词，时间比英国长得多-但原因完全不同。)
note_en:
I have been a happy man ever since January 1, 1990, when I no longerhad an email address. I'd used email since about 1975, and it seemsto me that 15 years of email is plenty for one lifetime.
 Email is a wonderful thing for people whose role in life is to be on topof things. But not for me; my role is to be on the bottom of things.What I do takes long hours of studying and uninterruptible concentration.I try to learn certain areas of computer science exhaustively; then I tryto digest that knowledge intoa form that is accessible to people who don't have time for such study.
 On the other hand, I need to communicate with thousands of people all overthe world as I write my books. I also want to be responsive to the peoplewho read those books and have questions or comments. My goal isto do this communication efficiently, in batch mode --- like, one day everysix months. So if you want to write to me about any topic, please usegood ol' snail mail and send a letter to the following address:
  I have a wonderful secretary who looks at the incoming postal mail and separatesout anything that she knows I've been looking forward to seeing urgently.Everything else goes into a buffer storage area, which I empty periodically.
 My secretary also prints out all nonspam email messages addressed to taocp@cs.stanford.edu or  knuth-bug@cs.stanford.edu,so that I can reply with written comments when I have a chance. If I runacross such a message that was misaddressed --- I mean, if the messageasks a question instead of reporting an error --- I try not to get angry.I used to just throw all such sheets in the wastebasket.But now I save them for scratch paper, so thatI can print test material for  The Art of Computer Programming onthe blank sides. I DO NOT ANSWER UNSOLICITED EMAILS, nor do I respond toemails that were sent to my colleagues with a bothersome request for them tocommunicate with me. Email is a no-no EXCEPT for reporting errors in books.
 You might also try faxing me at 650-725-4671. But be warned that I lookat incoming fax mail last, perhaps only once every twelve months instead of six.
 `I don't even have an e-mail address. I have reached an age where my mainpurpose is not to receive messages.' --- Umberto Eco, quoted in the New Yorker
 Sometimes I do send email, through my secretary, with respect to theproject I'm currently working on, when I believe that the recipientwon't be bothered by my request. But I hope you can understand why Iam almost always unhappy to receive unsolicited email myself.Thank you for your patience and cooperation as I try to finish The Art of Computer Programming( TAOCP),a work that I began in 1962 and that I will need many years to complete.In return, I promise not to send unwelcome email requests to you.
  Newly coined nonce words of English are often spelled with a hyphen, but thehyphen disappears when the words become widely used. For example, peopleused to write ``non-zero'' and ``soft-ware'' instead of ``nonzero''and ``software''; the same trend has occurred for hundreds ofother words. Thus it's high time for everybody to stop using the archaicspelling ``e-mail''. Think of how many keystrokes you will save inyour lifetime if you stop now! The form ``email'' has been wellestablished in England for several years, so I am amazed to seeAmericans being overly conservative in this regard. (Of course,``email'' has been a familiar word in France, Germany, and the Netherlandsmuch longer than in England --- but for an entirely different reason.)

note:
在4月份企稳后，韩国确诊的新冠肺炎病例在过去几周再次开始攀升。根据约翰·霍普金斯医科大学的数据，截至8月27日，过去一个月记录的新增病例为4503例，死亡13例，使全国确诊病例总数达到18706例，死亡人数达到313例。
该国此前通过广泛的检测、接触者追踪和行动限制令，基本上能够控制疾病的传播。
但最近的疫情-韩国总统文在寅称其为“冠状病毒进入我国以来最大的危机”-意味着韩国将恢复对新冠肺炎的应急系统，这可能意味着更严格的社会疏远命令，将所有集会限制在10人以内。
今年6月，三星电子韩国一家研究中心关闭，1,200名员工被要求在家工作，因为一名员工的孩子新冠肺炎检测呈阳性。今年4月，该公司表示，已经为其全球员工建立了地区性响应团队，并“强烈建议员工尽可能在家工作”。
note_en:
After stabilizing in April, confirmed COVID-19 cases in South Korea have begun climbing again over the past few weeks. According  to data from John Hopkins University of Medicine, as of August 27, there had been 4,503 cases new cases and 13 deaths recorded over the past month, bringing the total number of confirmed cases in the country to 18,706 and deaths to 313.
 The country had previously been able to  mostly contain the spread of the disease through widespread testing, contact tracing, and movement restriction orders.
 But the recent outbreak, which South Korean president Moon Jae-in described as “the biggest crisis since coronavirus came into our country,” means the country  will reinstate its emergency-response system for COVID-19, which may mean stricter social-distancing orders that limit all gatherings to 10 people.
 In June, one of Samsung Electronics South Korean research centers was closed and  1,200 employees were ordered to work from home after an employee’s child tested positive for COVID-19. In April, the  company said it had established regional response teams for its workforce around the world and “strongly advised employees to work from home where possible.”
title: 据报道，三星将测试远程工作计划，因为韩国正在应对新的新冠肺炎病例
title_en: Samsung will reportedly test remote work program as South Korea copes with new COVID-19 cases

note:
越来越多的卫星发射到地球轨道上的“巨型卫星”将污染天文学家收集的数据，并深刻改变人类对夜空的看法。这是对亚马逊(Amazon)和太空探索技术公司(SpaceX)等公司推出的这些卫星网络可能会如何影响地球天文观测所做的最详细评估得出的结论。
从望远镜凝视天空的角度来看，卫星可以反射阳光，看起来像明亮的条纹。这些条纹干扰了天文观测，包括基础物理和宇宙学的研究，太阳系以外的行星，以及威胁地球的小行星。8月25日，美国天文学会和NOIRLab(由美国国家科学基金会(NSF)资助的几个美国天文台的伞形组织)在一份8月25日的报告中表示：“一些现象肯定会因为重大干扰而无法被发现。”
天文学家可以采取一些措施来减少影响，比如安排观测以避免卫星飞过的时间，或者与卫星运营商合作开发反射率较低的版本。但根据这份报告，捕捉阳光的高空卫星是一个特别的问题。
加州大学戴维斯分校的物理学家托尼·泰森(Tony Tyson)说：“在这样一个卫星星座的半夜里，没有地方可以躲避。”
预计未来几年将有数万颗新卫星飞入太空，为全球人民提供宽带互联网。位于加利福尼亚州霍桑的航空航天公司SpaceX已经发射了计划中的12000颗Starlink卫星中的650多颗。其他运营商包括总部位于伦敦的OneWeb和亚马逊(Amazon)。OneWeb预计将发射4.8万颗卫星，其中74颗已经发射。亚马逊上月获得美国政府批准，将为其计划中的Kuiper服务发射3236颗卫星。
SpaceX、OneWeb和亚马逊都在与天文学家就如何减轻卫星在夜空中的明亮现象进行谈判。科学家们直到去年6月才意识到这个问题，当时SpaceX推出了第一组Starlink，很快就出现了专业天文图像上拖曳的明亮条纹。从那时起，天文学家们就一直在努力弄清楚这个问题会有多糟糕，以及他们能做些什么。这份新报告是迄今为止对问题严重程度的最技术性评估。它描述了6月份一次研讨会的结果，这次研讨会汇集了天文学家、卫星运营商、暗天爱好者和其他人。
NOIRLab天文学家、撰写这份报告的团队联合主席康妮·沃克(Connie Walker)表示，避免对地面可见光和红外望远镜造成任何影响的唯一方法是发射零颗卫星。
鉴于许多巨型星座已经获得批准，一些已经开始发射，天文学家们正在试图弄清楚如何适应这些明亮的条纹。受影响最大的是大范围的天文观测--特别是那些将由维拉·鲁宾天文台(Vera Rubin Observatory)进行的观测。维拉·鲁宾天文台是美国国家科学基金会(NSF)在智利塞罗帕松(Cerro Pachón)山顶建造的8.4米高的望远镜，泰森是该望远镜的首席科学家。从2022年开始，该设施将在10年内每隔几天拍摄覆盖整个可见天空的广域图像，以构建宇宙如何随时间变化的图片。
在日落和日出前后的几个小时里，这些条纹对于地面观测来说是最糟糕的，因为那时卫星捕捉到了太阳的微光，还没有在地球的阴影下。这损害了在这几个小时内进行的研究，比如搜索近地小行星。今年早些时候发表的模拟结果为新报告的结论提供了依据，详细描述了鲁宾天文台在黄昏和黎明前后进行的30%-40%的曝光可能会受到怎样的影响。
根据这份报告，对于在高海拔(离地面600公里以上)运行的卫星来说，这个问题尤其严重，因为这些卫星远离地球的阴影，在一些地方整夜都可以看到。OneWeb计划中的巨型星座在地球上空1200公里的轨道上运行，就属于这一类。
OneWeb的代表已经与美国天文学会会面，开始讨论缓解这一问题的方法。英国航天局是OneWeb今年早些时候宣布破产后接管该公司的财团的一部分，目前正在与皇家天文学会(Royal Astronomical Society)进行类似的谈判。亚马逊还一直在与天文学社区的代表讨论它可能会做的事情。
对于卫星的亮度或暗度，没有国家或国际的规定。新报告建议天文学家和卫星运营商继续合作，开发评估和降低卫星亮度的方法。
SpaceX公司一直走在这一领域的前列，主要是因为它是第一家开始发射这么多这样的新卫星的公司。8月18日发射的最新一组Starlink；今年剩余时间计划再发射5次，每次大约有60颗卫星。黄昏时分，一旦由大约12000个星链组成的完整星座发射升空，从黑暗天空中抬头看去的人可能会同时看到数百个星链。
SpaceX采取的降低卫星能见度的第一步之一是在发射后不久将它们定向到不同的方向，以便它们向地面反射较少的阳光。该公司还将一款Starlink漆成黑色，并于1月份推出，看看这是否会有所帮助。但SpaceX负责卫星政府事务的副总裁帕特里夏·库珀(Patricia Cooper)在最近的一次网络研讨会上表示，黑色油漆使卫星变得过热，损害了其互联网运营。因此，SpaceX在4月份开始发射带有遮阳板的Starlink，以阻挡阳光，否则太阳光会从其天线上反射出来，一旦到达最终位置，天线就是Starlink亮度的主要来源。
到目前为止，这些“游客卫星”星链中只有一颗完成了到达地球上空550公里的最终轨道的旅程。业余天文学家报告说，遮阳板似乎使星链比没有遮阳伞时更暗。但西雅图华盛顿大学的天文学家梅雷迪思·罗尔斯(Meredith Rawls)表示，天文学家们仍在等待进一步证实这些修改是否有显著影响。无论如何，SpaceX现在正在推出其所有带有遮阳面罩的Starlink。
天文学家还可以通过将观测安排在卫星经过头顶的时间附近，或者使用图像清理软件来去除污染，从而避免一些卫星条纹。但泰森说，前者需要卫星运营商提供关于卫星在任何特定时间将在哪里的非常精确的信息，而后者不能恢复在被剥离3的像素中丢失的基础科学。
卫星巨型星座还会干扰地面射电望远镜的观测，并导致太空拥堵。NOIRLab和美国天文学会正计划在明年举办一次研讨会，评估围绕巨型星座的政策和监管问题。
杰森顾问小组，一个由美国科学家和工程师组成的有影响力的秘密组织，也在为NSF撰写一份关于卫星巨型星座和天文学的报告。
note_en:
‘Megaconstellations’ of satellites increasingly launching into orbit around Earth will contaminate the data astronomers collect — and profoundly shift humanity's view of the night skies. That's the conclusion of the most detailed assessment yet of how these satellite networks, launched by companies including Amazon and SpaceX, might affect astronomical observations from Earth.
 From the perspective of a telescope gazing at the sky, satellites can reflect sunlight and appear as bright streaks. These streaks interfere with astronomical observations including studies of fundamental physics and cosmology, planets beyond the Solar System, and asteroids that threaten Earth. “Some phenomena will surely go undiscovered as a result of significant interference,” says  a 25 August report from the American Astronomical Society and NOIRLab, an umbrella organization for several US observatories that is funded by the National Science Foundation (NSF).
  Astronomers can take some steps to reduce the impact, such as scheduling observations to avoid times when satellites whiz by, or working with satellite operators to develop versions that are less reflective. But high-flying satellites that catch the sunlight are a particular problem, according to the report.
 “There’s no place to hide in the middle of the night from such a satellite constellation,” says Tony Tyson, a physicist at the University of California, Davis.
  Tens of thousands of new satellites are expected to soar into space in the coming years, to provide broadband internet to people around the globe. SpaceX, an aerospace company in Hawthorne, California, has already launched more than 650 of a planned 12,000 Starlink satellites. Other operators include the London-based OneWeb, which has launched 74 of what it hopes will be a gigantic fleet of 48,000 satellites, and Amazon, which last month received US government approval to launch 3,236 satellites for its planned Kuiper service.
 SpaceX, OneWeb and Amazon have all been in talks with astronomers about how to mitigate the satellites' bright appearance in the night sky. Scientists only realized the problem last June after SpaceX launched its first set of Starlinks, which soon appeared as bright streaks trailing across professional astronomical images. Astronomers  have been working ever since to figure out how bad the problem will be and what they can do about it. The new report is the most technical assessment to date of the extent of the problem. It describes the results of  a June workshop that brought together astronomers, satellite operators, dark-sky enthusiasts and others.
 The only way to avoid any impact on ground-based visible-light and infrared telescopes would be to launch zero satellites, says Connie Walker, an astronomer at NOIRLab and co-chair of the team that wrote the report.
  Given that many of the megaconstellations have already been approved and some have started launching, astronomers are trying to figure out how to live with the bright streaks. Most affected are wide-field sky surveys — especially those set to be conducted by the Vera Rubin Observatory, an 8.4-metre telescope that the NSF is building atop the Chilean mountain of Cerro Pachón and for which Tyson is the chief scientist. Starting in 2022, the facility will take wide-field images that cover the entire visible sky every few days for 10 years to build a picture of how the Universe changes over time.
 The streaks are worst for ground observations in the hours around sunset and sunrise, when satellites catch the gleam of the Sun and are not yet in Earth’s shadow. This harms research that is done in these hours, such as searches for near-Earth asteroids. Simulations published earlier this year  1 fed into the new report’s conclusions, detailing how 30–40% of exposures made at the Rubin observatory around twilight and dawn could be affected.
 The problem is particularly acute for satellites that orbit at high elevations — more than 600 kilometres above ground — where they remain out of Earth's shadow and visible all night in some locations, according to the report. Orbiting at 1,200 kilometres above Earth, OneWeb’s planned megaconstellation would fall into this category.
  OneWeb representatives have met with the American Astronomical Society to begin discussing ways to mitigate the problem. The UK space agency, which is part of a consortium that took over OneWeb after the company declared bankruptcy earlier this year, is in similar discussions with the Royal Astronomical Society. Amazon has also been talking with representatives from the astronomical community about what it might do.
  There are no national or international regulations on how bright or dim satellites should be. The new report recommends that astronomers and satellite operators continue to work together to develop methods for assessing and reducing satellite brightness.
 SpaceX has been at the forefront of this, mostly because it was the first company to start launching so many of these new satellites. The most recent set of Starlinks launched on 18 August; five more launches, of roughly 60 satellites each, are planned for the rest of the year. Around twilight, once the full constellation of around 12,000 Starlinks is launched, hundreds of them may be visible at once to people looking up from dark-sky sites  2.
 One of the first steps SpaceX took to reduce the satellites’ visibility was to orient them in a different direction soon after launch so that they reflect less sunlight to the ground. The company also painted one Starlink black and launched it in January to see whether that would help. But the black paint made the satellite thermally ‘hot’, harming its internet operations, said Patricia Cooper, SpaceX’s vice-president of satellite government affairs, at a recent webinar. So in April, SpaceX instead began launching Starlinks with a sunshade to block sunlight that would otherwise reflect off its antennas, which are the main source of the Starlinks’ brightness once they have reached their final positions.
 So far, only one of these ‘VisorSat’ Starlinks has finished the journey to its final orbit of 550 kilometres above Earth. Amateur astronomers have reported that the sunshade appears to make the Starlink fainter than it would otherwise be. But astronomers are still awaiting further confirmation that the modifications have a significant effect, says Meredith Rawls, an astronomer at the University of Washington in Seattle. Regardless, SpaceX is now launching all of its Starlinks with the sun-shielding visor.
  Astronomers might also be able to avoid some of the satellite streaks by scheduling their observations around the time when a satellite is passing overhead, or using image-cleaning software to strip out the contamination. But the former requires very precise information from satellite operators about where a satellite will be at any particular time, and the latter cannot restore the fundamental science lost in the pixels that were stripped out  3, Tyson says.
 Satellite megaconstellations can also interfere with observations by radio telescopes on the ground and contribute to congestion in space. NOIRLab and the American Astronomical Society are planning a workshop for next year to assess the policy and regulation issues surrounding megaconstellations.
 The JASON advisory group, an influential and secretive set of US scientists and engineers, is also working on a report on satellite megaconstellations and astronomy for the NSF.
title: 卫星“巨型星座”将如何对天文图像进行光弹
title_en: How satellite ‘megaconstellations’ will photobomb astronomy images
