具体的支票金额尚未披露，但我们知道的是，Psision Capital合伙人艾琳·伯比奇已经加入了Fronted的董事会。这并不令人惊讶，因为前面的联合创始人西蒙·万斯-科利纳是Monzo的早期和重要员工，Monzo是挑战者Back的挑战者，Psience Capital和Burbige是Monzo的原始支持者。
前面的联合创始人兼首席执行官杰米证实了激情资本的投资，他让TechCrunch发表了以下声明：
与我们在大流行后的世界站稳脚跟的许多企业一样，我们很感激有像Psision Capital这样的支持投资者，他们从一开始就支持我们，并相信我们帮助租房者搬家的愿景。
该公司去年年底由坎贝尔、万斯-柯林纳和安东尼·曼创立-他们分别是巴德、蒙佐和苹果的前雇员。该公司计划在今年晚些时候推出金融科技的产品，帮助租房者为他们的租金押金融资。
这家新成立的公司目前正处于FCA“沙盒”计划(由英国金融服务监管机构运营)中，开始放贷只能用于租金押金的现金。
通过使用开放银行和其他金融技术，并提供一种旨在直接为存款融资的信贷产品，Fronted相信，它可以比现有选择(如信用卡、发薪日贷款人和透支，或保险支持的会员计划)更便宜地放贷，而且风险更低。
去年年底，坎贝尔和万斯-科利纳解释说，申请使用前台服务的租户将被要求使用开放银行将他们的银行联系起来，从而分享他们最近的交易数据，并提供他们希望租用的房产的详细信息。然后，一旦Front运行了所需的检查并同意提供信贷，初创公司将直接将资金寄给房地产经纪人，放入英国的存款保护计划，这意味着贷款永远不会触及租房者的手(或钱包)。
然后，租房者将按设定的时间表偿还贷款，或者当他们有钱时，他们可以完全还清贷款。还有一个计划中的“假期模式”，将允许借款人暂时减少每月还款，以帮助避免陷入财务困境.。
随着冠状病毒大流行的蔓延和最不确定的时候，Fronted暂停了运营，但随着最初的产品制造和银行资金的投入，推出看起来不会太远。
坎贝尔补充说：“我们正处于(监管批准)的最后阶段，一旦获得授权，我们就可以推出。”
note_en:
The exact cheque size isn’t yet disclosed, but what we do know is that Passion Capital partner Eileen Burbidge has joined Fronted’s board. That’s unsurprising, given that Fronted co-founder Simon Vans-Colina was an early and important employee of Monzo, the challenger back of which Passion Capital and Burbidge are original backers.
 Confirming Passion Capital’s investment, Fronted co-founder and CEO Jamie have TechCrunch the following statement:
  “Like a lot of businesses we have been finding our feet in post-pandemic world, we are grateful to have supporting investors like Passion Capital who have supported us from the very beginning and who believe in our vision to help renters move”.
 The company,  founded late last year by Campbell, Vans-Colina and Anthony Mann — former employees at Bud, Monzo and Apple, respectively — is planning to launch later this year with a fintech product to help renters finance their rental deposits.
 The nascent company is currently in the FCA “sandbox” program (run by the U.K. financial services regulator) to begin lending cash that can only be used for a rental deposit.
 By using open banking and other financial technology, and offering a credit product designed to finance deposits directly, Fronted believes it can lend more cheaply than existing options — such as credit cards, pay-day lenders and overdrafts, or insurance-backed membership schemes — and at lower risk.
 Late last year, Campbell and Vans-Colina  explained that renters that apply to use the Fronted service will be asked to link their bank using open banking, therefore sharing their recent transaction data, and provide details of the property they wish to rent. Then, once Fronted has run the required checks and agreed to provide credit, the startup will send the money directly to the estate agent to be placed in the U.K.’s Deposit Protection Scheme, meaning that the loan never touches the renter’s hands (or wallet).
 Renters will then pay back the loan over a set schedule, or they can pay it off entirely when they have the money to do so. There is also a planned “holiday mode” that will allow borrowers to temporarily reduce their monthly payments in order to help avoid falling into financial difficulty.
 Fronted paused operations as the coronavirus pandemic took hold and at the height of uncertainty, but with the initial product built and money in the bank, a launch doesn’t look too far off.
 “We are in the final stage [of regulatory approval] and once we are authorised we can launch,” adds Campbell.
title: 激情资本支持了Fronted，这是一家希望提供贷款来支付租金押金的初创公司
title_en: Passion Capital has backed Fronted, the startup that wants to offers loans to cover rent deposits

note:
向后兼容的硬件单元意味着，去年Fairphone3的用户只需换出几个模块就可以手持Fairphone3+，而不是购买一整台新设备。
Fairphone在其早期型号的“道德智能手机”上也取得了类似的成就，但这一次它设法缩短了为其最新一代设备提供“即插即用”升级模块所需的时间。
Fairphone联合创始人巴斯·范·阿贝尔(Bas Van Abel)表示：“我们所能做的就是将即插即用的整体理念带给智能手机业务中的消费者。”“这一部分不是微不足道的，因为你必须想象一下，把所有东西都放到那个模块里，然后把它放到旧的手机…里。在以前的体系结构中，不仅硬件必须适合，所有东西都必须以正确的方式连接，软件也是如此。
“但我们已经能够做到这一点，而且花了一些时间，但我们做到这一点的速度比Fairphone2快得多。所以我们也为此感到自豪。”
他补充说：“最重要的是，这真的是向业界发出了一个信号，那就是有可能对你的手机进行升级，而不必每年都推出一款全新的手机。”
找到延长设备寿命的聪明方法是Fairphone使命的核心。与智能手机消费相关的最大资源坑是每年或两年一次的升级周期，这鼓励消费者将功能完善的手机更换为闪亮的新机型。Fairphone3的拥有者可以更干净地获得最新的套件。
Fairphone正在为目前的Fairphone3用户单独销售Fairphone3+摄像头和音频模块-初始价格为70欧元，直到9月底(从10月份起上涨到~95欧元)。
该公司还在销售一款Fairphone 3+手机，建议零售价为469欧元，针对的是该品牌的新用户-从今天开始在其网站上和通过合作零售商开启预售，整个欧洲的发布日期为9月14日。
规格方面，4G Fairphone 3+拥有5.7英寸全高清显示屏，宽高比为18：9，由高通骁龙632芯片组驱动。它开箱即用，运行的是Android 10。机上有4 GB的RAM和64 GB的ROM，可以通过microSD进行扩展。可拆卸电池为3000mAh。还有蓝牙5.0、NFC和指纹扫描仪。
范·阿贝尔证实，该公司将继续出售去年的旗舰车型-但降价约为400欧元。
3+模块只能向后兼容一代Fairphone，这意味着任何仍在使用Fairphone2的人都无法获得这一即插即用升级。那里的拦截者是核心模块佩尔·范·阿贝尔(Per Van Abel)，他表示，对于能够跨越多代智能手机的模块化升级来说，无法将SOC换成升级的芯片组仍然是最大的挑战。
“我们的愿景是，您最终也可以替换核心模块…。调制解调器和处理器在哪里，“他说，并暗示这在”几年内“可能是可能的。
然而，更广泛的问题是，零部件行业的发展速度仍然如此之快，与Fairphone的长寿目标仍然相去甚远。这家社会企业承诺为其销售的每台设备提供长达5年的支持，这意味着它需要相关备件仍可用，才能提供更换或自己储存-这是一个资本密集型的过程。这与处理器制造商激烈的升级轨迹大相径庭。
从可持续性和资源的角度来看，对智能手机用户来说，最好的选择也是尽可能长时间地使用同一芯片组。智能手机市场的成熟和技术的商品化-导致我们现在通常看到的更多迭代的设备更新-也默许了这一点。
范·阿贝尔可以指出，消费者持有手机的平均时间大约是Fairphone启动时的两倍。这是一种趋势，为专注于创新生产寿命更长的智能手机的环境敏感品牌提供了提升。
他说：“我们已经对Fairphone3进行了生命周期评估，我们还测试了手机的哪些部分有什么样的足迹，你还可以看到，手机近80%的二氧化碳足迹都在SOC的制造和生产范围内。”“所以这意味着，如果你真的想从可持续发展的角度来看，尽可能长时间地保留手机的这一部分真的是有意义的。因为对自然的大部分伤害都在那部分。因此，即使更换那个部件--能够更换那个部件--这也很棒，但遗憾的是，我们在手机中扔掉了很多东西、模块和组件。“
他补充说：“目前在手机行业进行回收是非常愚蠢的。”“它是这样做的：你把手机收集起来，他们把它们放进烤箱里--然后把它们烧掉。”然后他们把矿物运出…。你仍然可以重复使用这些矿物，但这并不是什么聪明的事情。没有什么是真正重复使用的，所以所有的电容器，屏幕的玻璃…。因此，在某种程度上，您还可以像过去那样更换处理器，这在一定程度上是有意义的。“。
当我们去年回顾Fairphone3时，我们对这款Android设备的正常感觉印象深刻--掩盖了它模块化、可拆卸的内部结构，以及Fairphone多年来一直在仔细审查和改造供应链，以证明其大胆宣称的一款“敢于公平”的手机是正确的。
现在，随着Fairphone3+模块的推出，去年的手机相机硬件得到了提升-48MP主镜头和16MP前置镜头通过新模块取代了去年的1200万和800万(主模块和前置模块可以单独购买，也可以作为升级包购买)。
从表面上看，这在硬件上看起来是一个巨大的进步，但这要归功于使用三星GM1传感器的相机模块-它使用0.8微像素的微小像素来提供等于1.6微像素的光敏度。
因此，它实际上是一种软件技术，可以从硬件中获得更多好处，但也有一个代价，那就是它需要对图像质量进行一定程度的压缩。Fairphone的一位女发言人证实，主镜头的“有效输出”仍然是1200万像素。她补充说：“这是业界对三星S5KGM1、三星Galaxy A90 5G、诺基亚7.2和索尼IMX363等手机的普遍做法。”
正如我们在去年9月对Fairphone3的评估中所指出的那样，2019年的旗舰产品拍摄了相当标准的照片-照片质量接近可接受的水平，而不是脱颖而出。与高端智能手机市场的性能差距是显而易见的，尽管Fairphone与其早期手机相比性能已经大幅提升。
该公司似乎热衷于进一步缩小照片质量差距。现在，它吹捧通过3+升级“显著”改善了照片和视频质量-它说这支持“更清晰的自拍和更清晰的视频通话”。
它还做了优化软件的工作，注意到支持增强的对象跟踪，更快的自动对焦和图像稳定，“以获得更可靠的拍摄”。而根据新闻稿，新的音频模块提供的是“更响亮、更清晰的声音”。
考虑到相机对智能手机用户的核心地位，专注于提升照片和视频性能是有意义的-这将推动TikTok等时尚社交视频分享应用的崛起。
成功地说服消费者延长现有手机的使用时间，意味着关注这类应用程序的趋势，以确保硬件和软件与人们使用手机的方式保持同步。
对于Fairphone3+手机的购买者来说，还有另一个改进：它号称使用了40%的回收塑料-而去年的机型只有9%。Fairphone说，回收塑料的体积现在相当于一个33毫升的塑料饮料瓶-所以(目前)这是防止一件塑料垃圾最终流入大海。
虽然有些人可能会想，一个可持续的智能手机品牌在去年旗舰手机开箱仅一年后就推出了一款新机型，这是否存在微妙的矛盾，但范·阿贝尔表示，扩大产品组合非常重要-这是增长对符合道德的智能手机需求的总体使命的一部分。
反过来，这种需求又需要为批发升级到循环经济所需的那种全行业转变建立动力。以及将设备作为服务提供的潜力。
他告诉TechCrunch：“我们想卖出尽可能多的手机--因为我们的使命是展示人们对合乎道德的手机的需求。”“所以我们卖的手机越多，就越能表明需求确实存在。但这也在寿命方面造成了问题，所以我们有另一个KPI，我们说我们希望人们尽可能长时间地使用我们的手机-所以我们衡量人们实际使用我们的手机的时间，这一点每年都在改善。因此，作为Fairphone的一名销售人员，他们得到了一项非常艰难的任务，因为他们必须尽可能多地销售手机，但他们不能接近已经拥有手机的人。“。
他补充说：“我们正在挑战自己，尽可能地将商业模式与这些资源脱钩，但由于我们在业务核心接受了这一挑战，我认为我们也走在了行业需要迈向的方向。”
“任何人都不能忽视这样一个事实，我们的资源正在耗尽，而且越来越难获得这些资源。例如，看看钴。锂离子电池。现在钴已经快用完了。现在它的价格是以前的10倍、20倍--因为我们需要各种各样的电池来进行能量转换。因此，即使是可持续发展也需要这些资源，而这些资源是你不能纯粹从循环利用中获得的。所以我们知道这种情况必须改变。即使出于地缘政治原因，我认为我们的所作所为也迫使我们走在了游戏的前列。“
据范·阿贝尔说，过去十年来，对Fairphone的需求一直在稳步增长，这家社会企业现在“几乎”实现了盈利。“我们已经售出了超过20万部手机，其中6万部是Fairphone 1。我们已经卖出了10万台Fairphone2。去年我们售出了近50000部Fairphone3，今年我们的目标是超过10万部Fairphone3+，“他说。
“我们从来没有投资组合。现在我们实际上有了两款手机产品组合，Fairphone 3和3+，因为我们将以较低的价格销售3和旧模块-以前的模块-以及3+和新模块。因此，对于不需要最新相机改进的人，我们也有一个价格点。“。
Fairphone在很大程度上仍然是一个欧洲项目-一个完全处于有利地位的项目，可以在未来几年受益于泛欧盟推动的可持续发展和循环经济。(欧盟委员会关于手机的“维修权”提案看起来肯定是有帮助的。)。
根据范·阿贝尔的说法，就目前而言，Fairphone最大的市场仍然是德国。尽管他表示，新投资组合的销售重点是推动德国的更多增长，但法国、荷兰和英国是其继续关注的其他主要市场。“我们的目标也更多地瞄准了斯堪的纳维亚半岛，”他补充道。
“商品化行业的危险在于，你可以轻松、廉价地获得所有这些技术，你会看到它正朝着两个方向发展：高端和真正的低端产品。但我希望消费者也会重视公司本身，以及品牌和它们所代表的东西。然而(iPhone制造商)苹果代表设计；他们有溢价-你买的不仅仅是手机。我认为Fairphone也有这一点。
“我们有一个令人信服的故事。尤其是你可以看到，在我读到的每一份报告中，有意识的消费群体都在不断增长。你可以看到它每年都在稳步增长。因此，人们确实更关注他们实际购买的东西。“。
在融资方面，这家社会企业拥有几年前从Impact投资者那里筹集的债务、股权和增长融资，处于有利地位。尽管范·阿贝尔提出了接受更多资金用于营销并帮助其保持规模的可能性。
“但目前我们做得很好，”他补充道。“受影响的投资者非常有耐心。这与公司的使命不谋而合。我认为人们真的是Fairphone的一部分-参与这家公司，因为他们不仅相信现金回报，而且相信影响。“
他还指出，Fairphone还在为供应链中的一些相关举措提供单独的融资，这些举措需要支持其声称的公平和道德的电子产品。
“我们刚刚成立的公平钴联盟就是一个很好的例子，”他说。他说：“我们对此感到非常自豪。我们已经与矿业公司、炼油厂和Signify这样的大公司建立了一个伟大的联盟，这些公司是钴供应链的一部分。它的部分资金也是由荷兰政府提供的。因此，我们有更多的经纪人职位-这就是作为一家社会企业的好处。有时你可能介于非营利和营利性部门之间。你可以很容易地在这两个世界之间架起桥梁。“
note_en:
The backwards compatible hardware units mean users of last year’s  Fairphone   3 only need swap out a few modules to be holding the Fairphone 3+ in their hand instead of buying a whole new device.
 Fairphone pulled off a similar feat with an earlier model of its ‘ethical smartphone’ but this time it’s managed to shrink the time it took it to offer ‘plug and play’ upgrade modules for its latest gen device.
  “What we’ve been able to do is get that whole idea of plug and play to the consumer within the smartphone business,” says Fairphone co-founder  Bas van Abel  . “That part is not trivial because you have to imagine that getting everything into that module and being able to put it into the old phone… Not only the hardware has to fit and everything has to connect in the right way in that previous kind of architecture but also the software.
 “But we’ve been able to do that, and it took some time but we’ve done it way faster than we were able to do it with the Fairphone 2. So we’re proud of that as well.”
 “The most important part is it’s really also a signal towards the industry that it’s possible to do upgrades with your phone and not have to come out with a totally new phone every year,” he adds.
 Finding clever ways to extend device longevity is a core plank of Fairphone’s mission. The biggest resource sinkhole associated with smartphone consumption is the annual or biennial upgrade cycle which encourages consumers to swap perfectly functional phones for a shiny new model. Fairphone 3 owners can get its latest kit with a cleaner conscience.
 Fairphone is selling the Fairphone 3+ camera and audio modules separately for current Fairphone 3 users — at an initial cost of €70 until the end of September (rising to ~€95 from October).
 It is also selling a Fairphone 3+ handset for an RRP of €469, aimed at new to the brand users — opening up pre-sales from today on its website and via partner retailers, with a release date of September 14 across Europe.
 Specs wise, the 4G Fairphone 3+ has a 5.7in Full-HD display with an 18:9 aspect ratio and is powered by a  Qualcomm Snapdragon 632 chipset. Out of the box it runs Android 10. On board there’s 4GB of RAM and 64GB of ROM, expandable via  microSD. The removable battery is 3,000mAh. There’s also Bluetooth 5.0, NFC and a fingerprint scanner.
 van Abel confirms the business will continue to sell last year’s flagship — but at a reduced price of around €400.
 The 3+ modules are only backwards compatible one generation of Fairphone which means anyone still using a Fairphone 2 can’t get this plug and play upgrade. The blocker there is the core module, per van Abel, who says not being able to swap the SOC out for an upgraded chipset remains the biggest challenge for modular upgrades that are able to span more than one smartphone generation.
 “Our vision is definitely there that you can also eventually replace the core module… where the modem and the processor is,” he says, hazarding that it might be possible “within a couple of years”.
 However the wider issue is the component industry still moves so fast it remains way out of step with Fairphone’s goal of longevity. The social enterprise pledges to provide up to five years of support for each device it sells, meaning it needs relevant spare parts to still be available in order that it can offer replacements or else stockpile them itself — a capital intensive process. And one that’s at sharp odds with the blistering upgrade trajectory of processor manufacturers.
 From a sustainability and resource perspective, the best option is also for a smartphone user to keep using the same chipset for as long as possible. The maturity of the smartphone market and commoditization of the tech — leading to the more iterative device refreshes we generally see now — also tacitly supports that.
 van Abel can point to consumers holding onto a handset for an average of about double the time they did when Fairphone got started. It’s a drift that’s providing uplift to environmentally sensitive brand focused on innovating to produce smartphones with a longer lifespan.
 “We’ve done a lifecycle assessment on the Fairphone 3 and what comes out of that we’ve also tested what parts of the phone have what kind of footprint and you also see that almost 80% of the CO2 footprint of the phone is within the making and the production of the SOC,” he says. “So that means that if you really want to look at it from a sustainability perspective it really makes sense to keep that part of the phone just as long as possible. Because most of the harm on nature is on that part. So even replacing that part — being able to swap that part — it’s great but it’s kind of a shame that we throw away a lot of stuff and modules and components in the phone.”
 “Recycling in the phone business at the moment is plain stupid,” he adds. “How it’s done is you collect the phones and they put them in an oven — they burn them. And then they get the minerals out… You can still reuse the minerals but there’s nothing smart about that. Nothing really has been reused so all the capacitors, the glass of the screen… So it does make sense at a certain point to being also able to swap the processor like you were able to do with the computers in the old days.”
 When we  reviewed the Fairphone 3 last year we were impressed by how normal the Android device felt — belying its modular, deconstructable interior and all the  years of effort Fairphone has ploughed into scrutinising and reworking supply chains to be able to stand up its bold claim of a phone that “dares to be fair”.
 Now, with the launch of the Fairphone 3+ modules, last year’s handset is getting a boost to its camera hardware — with a 48MP main lens and a 16MP front-facing lens offered as replacements to last year’s 12MP and 8MP units via the new modules (the main and front modules can be purchased separately or as an upgrade bundle).
 On the surface that looks like a huge step up in hardware but it’s down to the camera module using the Samsung GM1 sensor — which uses tiny pixels of 0.8-micro to deliver light sensitivity equal to 1.6-micro pixels.
 So it’s actually a software technique to eke more out of the hardware, with a trade off in that it entails some compression of picture quality. A Fairphone spokeswoman confirmed the main lens’ “effective output” is still 12MP. “This is common practice in the industry with phones such as the Samsung S5KGM1, Samsung Galaxy A90 5G, Nokia 7.2 and the Sony IMX363,” she added.
 As we noted in our review of the Fairphone 3 last September, the 2019 flagship took a fairly standard snap — with photo quality closer to acceptable, than stand out. The performance gap vs the premium end of the smartphone market was noticeable, even as Fairphone had substantially bested performance vs its earlier handsets.
 The company looks keen to further shrink the photo quality gap. Now it touts “significantly” improved photo and video quality via the 3+ upgrade — which it says supports “sharper selfies and clearer video calls”.
 It’s also done work to optimize the software, noting support for enhanced object tracking, faster autofocus and image stabilization “for more reliable shots”. While the new audio module serves “louder, crisper sound”, per its press release.
 A focus on boosting photo and video performance makes sense given how central the camera has become for smartphone users — feeding into the rise of trendy social video sharing apps like TikTok.
 Successfully convincing consumers to hold onto their existing handset for longer means paying attention to such app trends to make sure hardware and software are keeping up with how people are using their phones.
 For buyers of the Fairphone 3+ handset there’s another improvement: It boasts 40% recycled plastics — up from just 9% in last year’s model. Fairphone says the volume of recycled plastics is now equivalent to a 33cl plastic drinking bottle — so that’s one piece of plastic waste prevented from ending up in the sea (for now).
 While some might wonder if there’s a subtle contradiction in a sustainable smartphone brand launching a new model only a year after unboxing last year’s flagship, van Abel says expanding the portfolio in important — as part of the overall mission to grow demand for ethical smartphones.
 That demand is in turn needed to build momentum for the kind of industry-wide shift required for a wholesale upgrade to a circular economy. And the potential of offering  devices as a services.
 “We want to sell as many phones as possible — because our mission is to show that there is a demand for ethical phones,” he tells TechCrunch. “So the more phones we sell the more we can show that the demand is really there. But that also makes a problem in terms of longevity so we have another KPI where we say we want people to use our phone as long as possible — so we measure how long people actually use our phones and that’s improving every year as well. So a sales person at Fairphone they get a very hard kind of assignment because they have to sell as many phones as possible but they can’t approach people that already have them.”
 “We’re challenging ourselves to disconnect the business model from these resources as much as possible but because we take that challenge in the core of our business I think we’re also ahead of where the industry needs to move towards,” he adds.
 “Nobody can neglect the fact that we’re running out of resources and it’s getting harder and harder to get these resources. Look at cobalt, for example. Lithium ion batteries. There’s a run on cobalt. It’s gone like 10x, 20x the price it used to be — because we have this energy transition that we need all kinds of batteries for. So even sustainability needs these resources that you can’t get purely from recycling. So we know that this has to change. Even for geopolitical reasons I think that what we’re doing forces us to be ahead of the game.”
 Demand for Fairphones has been building steadily over the past decade and the social enterprise is now “almost” at profitability, per van Abel. “We’ve sold over 200k phones — of which 60k were Fairphone 1s. We’ve sold over 100k Fairphone 2s. And last year we sold almost 50k Fairphone 3s and this year we’re aiming for over 100k Fairphone 3+,” he says.
 “We’ve never had a portfolio. Now we actually have a portfolio of two phones, Fairphone 3 and 3+, because we’re going to sell the 3 as well at a lower price with the older modules — the previous modules — and the 3+ with the new modules. So that we also have a price point for people that don’t need the newest camera improvements.”
 Fairphone remains very much a European project — one that’s perfectly positioned to benefit from a pan-EU push towards sustainability and a circular economy in the coming years. (A  ‘right to repair’ Commission proposal for mobiles certainly looks helpful.)
 For now, the biggest market for Fairphones is still Germany, per van Abel. While he says its focus for sales of the new portfolio is to push for more growth in Germany, with France, Holland and the UK its other main markets of continued focus. “We’re aiming more also at Scandinavia,” he adds.
 “The danger of a commoditizing industry is where you get a lot of easy, cheap access to all these technologies and you see it moving towards two sides: The high end and the really low end stuff. But I hope that customers will also value the companies themselves, and the brands and what they stand for. Whereas [iPhone maker] Apple stands for design; they have a premium to it — you buy something more than just the phone. And I think Fairphone has that as well.
 “We have a compelling story. Especially you see the group of conscious consuming growing within every report I read. You see it growing steadily each year. So people do take more notice of what they actually buy.”
 Funding wise, the social enterprise is comfortably positioned with the debt, equity and growth financing it raised a few years back from impact investors. Though van Abel moots the possibility of taking in more funding to put towards marketing and help it keep scaling.
 “But at the moment we’re good,” he adds. “The impact investors are very patient. It goes with the mission of the company. I think people really are part of Fairphone — participate in this company because they believe not only in the cash return but also in the impact.”
 He also notes that Fairphone is also doing separate financing for some related initiatives in the supply chain which are required to underpin its claim of fair and ethical electronics.
 “A good example of that is the fair cobalt alliance that we’ve just set up,” he says. “We’re really proud of that. We have set up a great consortium with mining companies, with refineries, with big companies like  Signify, that are part of that supply chain of cobalt. It’s partly funded, as well, by the Dutch government. So we have more of a broker position — and that is the nice thing about being a social enterprise. You sometimes can be in between the non-profit and the for-profit sector. You can bridge easily those two worlds.”
title: Fairphone的新旗舰机型3+模块化升级仅需70欧元
title_en: Fairphone’s new flagship, the 3+, costs just €70 as a modular upgrade

note:
亚马逊将在其新的食品杂货连锁店开设第一家门店，旨在吸引与全食超市(Whole Foods)购物者不同的一群顾客。
亚马逊公司周四宣布，位于洛杉矶伍德兰山附近的第一家亚马逊生鲜杂货店将于本周向当地邀请的顾客开放。这家商店将在未来几周向更广泛的公众开放。亚马逊还有另外三个生鲜门店，包括加利福尼亚州的欧文和北岭，以及芝加哥的内珀维尔社区，所有这些门店都用于在线订单履行。该公司拒绝透露它们将于何时向公众开放。
亚马逊的目标是通过推出Fresh商店打开美国杂货业的一个新领域，使这家电商巨头与克罗格百货(Kroger Inc)和艾伯森百货(Albertsons)等连锁店展开更直接的竞争。新的模式融合了店内购物和在线购物，同时为消费者提供了比全食超市(Whole Foods)更低价位的各种产品。全食超市(Whole Foods)于2017年被亚马逊以137亿美元的价格收购，该公司以专注于有机产品而闻名，并不销售含有人造成分的产品。
这家35000平方英尺的店面大致相当于一家全食超市(Whole Foods)的大小，将储备纳贝斯科饼干、可口可乐饮料和家乐氏谷类食品等超市主食，以及全食超市更便宜的自有品牌365的产品。商店过道还将展示亚马逊的自有品牌，包括Happy Belly小吃、草书葡萄酒和一系列新的新鲜品牌食品。
亚马逊生鲜商店副总裁杰夫·赫尔布林(Jeff Helering)表示：我们确实认为这些模式相辅相成，服务于不同的需求。全食超市(Whole Foods)是天然、有机和清洁食品的长期先驱和领导者。亚马逊的新鲜选择是相当不同的。我们看到它们紧挨着操作，我们很高兴能为顾客提供这两种产品中的一种选择。
亚马逊还为这家生鲜商店融入了一些独特的高科技元素。伍德兰山的位置将是亚马逊最近推出的Dash手推车的试验场，这种手推车允许购物者不用排队就能结账。然而，这家商店并没有为亚马逊的Just Walk Out技术提供巨大的摄像头和传感器网络，这项技术被用于无收银员的Go便利店和Go杂货店。
Fresh商店各处都设有售货亭，配有声控Echo Show智能显示屏，购物者可以向亚马逊的Alexa语音助手寻求帮助，帮助定位商店中的商品，或者就晚餐做什么提出建议。
这些商店的设计是为了平衡在线订购的消费者和那些仍然喜欢亲自购物的消费者的需求。有一个专门的区域，顾客可以在那里领取在线订购的亚马逊或杂货，或者免费退还包裹。
虽然亚马逊在商店里融入了在线购物的体验，但仍然有传统杂货体验的迹象，比如准备好的食品区，购物者可以在那里订购新鲜的披萨或三明治，以及一个海鲜柜台。
在冠状病毒大流行加速了本已举步维艰的实体零售商倒闭之际，亚马逊正在扩大实体店的版图。近几个月来，由于客流量下降、消费者支出减少以及转向网购，许多零售商步履蹒跚，一些零售商面临永久关闭门店的风险。
这场流行病甚至改变了亚马逊自己的生鲜杂货店计划。直到本周，亚马逊所有的生鲜商店都被用作暗店，或者是不对公众开放的地点，以满足他们的在线送货需求。在此之前，亚马逊的所有生鲜商店都被用作暗店，或者是不对公众开放的地点，以满足他们的在线送货需求。上个季度，亚马逊表示，第二季度在线食品杂货销售额同比增长两倍，食品杂货送货能力增加了160%以上。
赫布林说：这不是我们最初的计划，但我们认为，只有在那个特定的时间点，才能满足我们在网上看到的高客户需求，这是最好的做法。
亚马逊也根据疫情调整了店铺的运营。它将要求顾客和员工在商店里戴口罩，并将商店的容量限制在50%，以及其他安全协议。
note_en:
Amazon is opening the first location of its new chain of grocery stores, which are designed to court a different set of customers than Whole Foods shoppers.
 The first Amazon Fresh grocery store, located in Los Angeles' Woodland Hills neighborhood, will open its doors this week to customers invited from the local area, the company announced Thursday. The store will open to the broader public in the coming weeks. Amazon has three other Fresh store locations, including in Irvine and Northridge, California, as well as Chicago's Naperville neighborhood, all of which are being used for online order fulfillment. The company declined to say when they'll open to the public.
  Amazon is aiming to crack open a new segment of the U.S. grocery industry with the launch of its Fresh store, bringing the e-commerce giant into more direct competition with chains like  Kroger and  Albertsons. The new format incorporates a blend of in-store and online shopping, while offering consumers a variety of products at lower price points than Whole Foods. Whole Foods, acquired by Amazon for $13.7 billion in 2017, is known for its focus on organic goods and doesn't sell products with artificial ingredients.
 The 35,000-square-foot store, which is roughly the size of a Whole Foods, will be stocked with supermarket staples like  Nabisco cookies,  Coca-Cola drinks and  Kellogg's cereal, as well as products from Whole Foods' cheaper private-label brand, 365. Store aisles will also feature Amazon's own brands, including Happy Belly snacks, Cursive wine and a new line of Fresh-branded food products.
 "We do think of these formats as complementing one another and serving different needs," said Jeff Helbling, vice president of Amazon Fresh stores. "Whole Foods is a longstanding pioneer and leader in natural, organic and clean foods. Amazon's Fresh selection is fairly different. We see them operating next to one another and we're excited to offer customers the choice between the two."
 Amazon has also incorporated some unique high-tech touches to the Fresh store. The Woodland Hills location will be the testing ground for Amazon's  recently launched Dash Carts, which allow shoppers to check out without waiting in line. However, the store doesn't feature the vast network of cameras and sensors powering Amazon's Just Walk Out technology, which is used in its cashierless Go convenience and Go Grocery stores.
 There are also kiosks located throughout the Fresh store equipped with voice-activated Echo Show smart displays, where shoppers can ask Amazon's Alexa voice assistant for help locating items in the store or for suggestions on what to make for dinner.
  The stores are designed to balance the needs of consumers who order online and those who still prefer to shop in person. There's a dedicated section where customers can pick up amazon.com or grocery orders placed online, or return their packages for free.
  While Amazon has incorporated the experience of shopping online in the store, there are still signs of the traditional grocery experience, like a prepared food section where shoppers can order fresh pizza or sandwiches, as well as a seafood counter.
 Amazon is expanding its footprint of physical stores at a time when the coronavirus pandemic has  accelerated the collapse of already struggling brick-and-mortar retailers. Many retailers have been hobbled in recent months by declining foot traffic, reduced consumer spending and a shift to online shopping, putting some at risk of permanent store closures.
 The pandemic even altered Amazon's own plans for its Fresh grocery stores. Until this week, all of Amazon's Fresh stores were used as "dark stores," or locations that are closed to the public in order to fulfill their needs for online deliveries. Last quarter, Amazon  said online grocery sales tripled year-over-year in the second quarter and it increased grocery delivery capacity by more than 160%.
 "It wasn't our initial plan, but we thought it was the best thing to do to serve the high customer demand we were seeing online only at that particular point in time," Helbling said.
 Amazon has also adjusted its operations in the store in light of the pandemic. It will require customers and employees to wear masks while in the store and it will limit the store to 50% capacity, among other safety protocols.
title: 亚马逊在洛杉矶开设了第一家生鲜杂货店，以挑选顾客，商店配备了Dash手推车和声控Echo Show显示屏

跳到主要内容史诗与苹果就堡垒之夜的未来展开的法律战很快演变成一场事关生死存亡的争夺战，争夺的是该游戏工作室最有利可图、最重要的资产之一：虚幻引擎平台。
在将堡垒之夜从应用商店中移除后，苹果将目标对准了Etic的其他与其游戏引擎捆绑在一起的开发者账户，威胁要切断对iOS和MacOS的支持，从而危及该公司的许可业务。EPIC周一晚间在最后一刻获得了临时限制令，以在短期内保护该业务，但虚幻引擎的命运仍悬而未决-危及依赖该引擎的整个第三方工具生态系统。
正如Epic上周在法庭动议中警告的那样，苹果针对虚幻引擎的行动将威胁到“数百款视频游戏、人脑、婴儿尤达和太空飞行”背后的软件。像曼达洛人这样的项目可能与Epic Games和堡垒之夜没有明显的联系，但它们依赖于公司及其工具来完成工作。随着Etic和苹果在多年的法律战中安顿下来，这类项目很可能也会被扰乱。
1998年与第一人称射击游戏“虚幻”一起开发的“虚幻引擎”已经成为史诗公司业务和整个游戏行业的基石。目前，数十家游戏制作商、好莱坞制作和特效制片厂以及3D渲染和计算机图形业务的其他公司都在使用它。
引擎也是史诗公司如何建立自己的游戏，包括堡垒之夜和过去的主要热门游戏，如战争机器和虚幻锦标赛。数十家大成本游戏制造商也放弃了为Epics定制的内部引擎，如Gearbox Software(Borderland 3)、Riot Games(Valorant)和Square Enix(最终幻想7翻拍)。EPIC将该软件授权给数千名没有资源构建自己的引擎的开发者，以换取对任何创建的引擎收取5%的版税。这一授权使Etic的虚幻引擎成为了一个流行的工具包，甚至对于大型、大预算的游戏也是如此，只有更专注于移动的Unity才能与之匹敌。
虚幻引擎有可能成为Etic与苹果之争的附带损害。
现在，在该公司在堡垒之夜应用内支付的争夺战中，该引擎有成为附带损害的风险。当苹果通过撤销Epic主应用开发者账号来删除堡垒之夜时，它还计划撤销Epic用来在iOS上维护虚幻引擎的开发者账号，这是其在违约后删除关联账号的政策的一部分。
法院已限制苹果在未来几周内撤销该账户，但是否会在整个审判期间保护该账户仍有待观察。(解决此事的临时禁令听证会定于9月28日举行。)。但是，如果苹果胜诉-无论是在初步禁令中，还是在更广泛的案件中-Etic针对虚幻引擎的测试工具将遭遇与堡垒之夜相同的命运，切断该系统的更新和更广泛的开发。
如果苹果在下个月成功抵挡了禁令，效果不会立竿见影，在虚幻引擎上运行的游戏将继续在iOS上运行-至少在出现重大漏洞之前是这样。但长期影响可能是灾难性的，特别是对iOS游戏开发商来说。由于苹果控制着开发者创建、测试、然后提交iOS应用程序和任何未来更新以供审查的流程，虚幻世界无法支持iOS平台，这意味着Etic无法整合操作系统的新功能，也无法实施游戏开发商可能需要的更新和错误修复，以保持产品的运行和功能。
Riley Testut是一位颇受欢迎的游戏仿真开发人员，他现在经营着一家名为AltStore的移动应用商店，他告诉The Verge：“即使Etic无法使用苹果的开发者工具，也不会破坏App Store(或其他平台)上使用虚幻的任何现有应用。”Riley Testut现在经营着一家名为AltStore的替代移动应用商店。“问题更多的是未来的开发者；如果Etic不能使用苹果的工具，他们就不能发布错误修复或新功能。这很可能意味着基于虚幻的游戏最终将在iOS和MacOS的未来版本中崩溃，而Etic将对此无能为力。
微软在上周提交的一份声明中在法庭上提出了类似的观点。“为不同的平台使用不同的游戏引擎开发一款游戏的成本和难度可能高得令人望而却步。无论如何，它都不如使用支持不同平台的游戏引擎那样划算，“微软负责游戏开发体验的总经理凯文·甘米尔在微软提交的支持EpicTM的声明中解释道。正如Gamill所描绘的那样，苹果终止Eic的账户将迫使其“在准备开发新游戏时，要么放弃iOS和MacOS平台上的客户和潜在客户，要么选择不同的游戏引擎。”
“就史诗而言，虚幻引擎实际上会被摧毁。它将不再是一个可用的引擎。“。
GamMill特别引用了微软的Forza Street，这是其Turn 10 Studios赛车专营权的移动版本，作为一个在虚幻引擎中依赖iOS支持的游戏的例子。他写道：“即使不确定虚幻引擎是否有能力继续支持iOS和MacOS，也会降低微软(我相信，还有其他游戏创作者)为他们的项目选择虚幻引擎的可能性。”当游戏创建者计划可能持续数年的开发项目时，重要的是要有信心，相信所选的引擎将继续在游戏创建者计划发行其游戏的所有平台上使用并提供支持。“。
虚幻引擎主要用于游戏机和PC游戏的开发，但随着游戏行业转向跨平台游戏，它的移动业务日益增长，这一业务变得越来越重要。EPIC特别吹捧一个与虚幻引擎协同工作的开发者工具包，称为Etic Online Services，它允许游戏利用最初为堡垒之夜开发的技术。该引擎的跨平台特性是一个卖点，使其成为构建虚拟世界的首选平台之一。如果没有苹果的开发者账户，Etic无法向对iOS感兴趣的开发者承诺任何这些好处，这是一个不受欢迎的冲击，因为它的主要竞争对手Unity正准备上市。
在周一的听证会上，Etic的一名律师辩称，即使是苹果公司威胁要终止与虚幻引擎相关的账户，也让开发人员“逃离”了该平台。“就史诗而言，虚幻引擎实际上会被摧毁。它将不再是一个可用的引擎，“克拉瓦特·斯瓦恩·摩尔律师事务所(Cravath，Swine&Amp；Moore)的凯瑟琳·福雷斯特(Katherine Forrest)辩称，该律师事务所在此案中代表Epic.。“如果史诗不能提供虚幻引擎，虚幻引擎将不复存在。我们收到开发人员的信息，说他们现在正在逃离虚幻引擎。“。
对于那些也在开发可以移植到多个平台的较小、图形密集度较低的项目(如独立游戏)的公司来说，缺乏对虚幻引擎的iOS支持将是交易的破坏者。对于游戏开发人员来说，切换到另一个引擎通常意味着放弃正在进行的项目的几乎每一个小时的工作，而完全从头开始。
对于使用实时渲染的好莱坞项目来说，虚幻引擎也变得越来越重要。特别值得一提的是，迪士尼和卢卡斯影业已经将Epic引擎作为一种电影制作工具，使用它为大型星球大战项目创建虚拟布景、人物和动画，包括索洛、盗贼一号和曼达洛人。
事实上，这部曼达洛人第一季的一半以上是使用定制的LED墙拍摄的，这些墙显示了虚拟布景，所有这些都由虚幻引擎提供动力，以便摄制组在后期制作和特效团队完成最终产品之前几周和几个月内实时看到镜头会是什么样子。迪士尼和卢卡斯电影公司没有回应就正在进行的诉讼可能会如何影响开发中的星球大战项目发表评论的请求。
奥迪(Audi)、保时捷(Porsche)和大众(Volkswagen)等汽车制造商也使用虚幻引擎来帮助设计汽车，使用可视化和其他软件流程。建筑公司出于类似的原因使用引擎，允许这些公司对正在进行的项目进行实时可视化。EPIC还授权Unreal用于广播和现场活动制作，该引擎能够生成实时图形、覆盖和其他实时渲染资产。
一个引人注目的例子是天气频道现在病毒式传播的混合现实片段，它融合了演播室里真实世界主持人周围模拟自然灾害的效果和视觉效果，所有这些都使用了虚幻引擎。
其中许多项目不需要iOS功能，但它们可能会在MacOS上运行后端系统，这将受到更有限的影响。如果虚幻引擎中没有MacOS支持，Etic将无法访问MacOS测试工具，也无法发布可能包含错误修复和安全补丁的快速更新。苹果现在还要求在MacOS上对第三方应用进行公证--换句话说就是苹果批准--只要他们有开发者ID。如果没有开发者ID或公证，虚幻引擎用户将不得不有效地寻找更新，并点击苹果强加给未经审查的第三方软件的安全警告。
“想象一下，失去对你的谷歌账户的访问，以及随之而来的一切，”一位iOS开发者告诉The Verge。由于担心负面评论苹果会产生影响，这位开发者不愿透露姓名。“苹果正在大举转向要求软件与苹果签署和公证的Mac，而Epicc将不再能够做到这一点。现在，试图在Mac上运行未签名的软件会显示可怕的警告，而且可以避开。在未来，就不那么确定了。“。
“这将是Mac上虚幻引擎产品线的终结。”
这位开发者说，就像iOS一样，失去MacOS支持的影响不会立即感受到，但风险可能会吓跑公司。“由于这种不确定性，开发者将不能再依赖虚幻引擎来支持苹果的平台。例如，Etic将不能发布他们的工具来支持Apple Silicon，因此这实际上将是Mac上虚幻引擎生产线的终结，“他们说。
现在就断言Etic的法律团队的言论-并声称虚幻引擎将“完全摧毁”-是夸张了，还是苹果真的有能力通过终止一个开发者账户，甚至只是威胁要这样做，来切断一家价值数十亿美元的公司的业务，现在还为时过早。但Epici承诺继续战斗，不会收回堡垒之夜的更新，正是这一更新开启了它与苹果前所未有的摊牌。现在很明显，苹果愿意尽其所能确保Epic不会开辟其他开发者可能热衷于追随的道路。
note_en:
Skip to main content                                                                                                                Epic’s legal fight with Apple over the future of  Fortnite has quickly evolved into an existential battle for one of the game studio’s most lucrative and important assets: the Unreal Engine platform.
 After removing  Fortnite from the App Store, Apple  targeted Epic’s other developer account tied to its game engine, putting at risk the company’s licensing business by threatening to cut iOS and macOS support. Epic secured a  last-minute temporary restraining order late Monday evening to protect the business in the short term, but the fate of the Unreal Engine is still up in the air — endangering an entire ecosystem of third-party tools that rely on the engine.
  As Epic warned  in a court motion last week, Apple’s moves against the Unreal Engine would threaten the software behind “hundreds of video games, the human brain, Baby Yoda, and space flight.” Projects like   The Mandalorian may have no obvious connection to Epic Games and  Fortnite, but they rely on the company and its tools to do their work. And as Epic and Apple settle in for years of legal warfare, those sorts of projects are likely to be disrupted too.
 Developed in 1998 alongside the first-person shooter  Unreal, the Unreal Engine has become a cornerstone of Epic’s business and the gaming industry at large. It’s currently used by dozens of game makers, Hollywood production and special effects studios, and other firms in the 3D rendering and computer graphics businesses.
 The engine is also how Epic builds its own games, including  Fortnite and past major hits like  Gears of War and  Unreal Tournament. Scores of big-budget game makers have also forgone custom, in-house engines for Epic’s, like Gearbox Software ( Borderlands 3), Riot Games ( Valorant), and Square Enix ( Final Fantasy VII Remake). Epic licensed the software to thousands of developers who don’t have the resources to build their own engine, in exchange for a five percent royalty on whatever’s created. That licensing has turned Epic’s Unreal Engine into a popular toolkit even for major, big-budget games, rivaled only by  the more mobile-focused Unity.
 The Unreal Engine is at risk of becoming collateral damage in Epic’s fight with Apple
 Now, the engine is at risk of becoming collateral damage in the company’s fight over in-app payments in  Fortnite. When Apple removed  Fortnite by revoking Epic’s primary app developer account, it also planned to revoke the developer account Epic uses to maintain the Unreal Engine on iOS, as part of its policy of removing linked accounts after a breach of contract.
 The court has restrained Apple from revoking that account for the next few weeks, but it remains to be seen whether it will keep the account protected for the full duration of the trial. (A temporary injunction hearing to settle the matter is scheduled for September 28th.) But if Apple prevails — either in the preliminary injunction or in the broader case — Epic’s testing tools for the Unreal Engine will suffer the same fate as  Fortnite, cutting the system off from updates and broader development.
  If Apple successfully fends off the injunction next month, the effect won’t be immediate, and games running on the Unreal Engine will keep working on iOS — at least until a major bug pops up. But the longer-term implications could be disastrous, especially for iOS game developers. Because Apple controls the process by which developers create, test, and then submit iOS apps and any future updates for review, Unreal’s inability to support the iOS platform means Epic can’t incorporate new features of the operating system or implement updates and bug fixes that a game developer might need to keep the product running and functional.
 “If Epic lost access to Apple’s developer tools, that wouldn’t break any current apps using Unreal in the App Store (or on other platforms),” Riley Testut, a popular game emulation developer who now operates an alternative mobile app store called AltStore, tells  The Verge. “The problem is more for developers going forward; if Epic can’t use Apple’s tools, they can’t release bug fixes or new features. This most likely would mean Unreal-based games would break eventually in future versions of iOS and macOS, and Epic would be unable to do anything about it.”
 Microsoft raised a similar point in court, in a declaration submitted last week. “Developing a game using different game engines for different platforms may be prohibitively expensive and difficult. In any event, it is not as cost-effective as using a game engine that supports different platforms,” explained Kevin Gammill, Microsoft’s general manager for gaming developer experiences, in a declaration of support for Epic that Microsoft filed. As Gammill paints it, Apple terminating Epic’s account would force it to “choose between abandoning its customers and potential customers on the iOS and macOS platforms or choosing a different game engine when preparing to develop new games.”
 “So far as Epic is concerned, the Unreal Engine will be actually destroyed. It will no longer be a usable engine.”
 Gammill specifically cited Microsoft’s  Forza Street, a mobile version of its Turn 10 Studios racing franchise, as an example of a game that relies on iOS support in the Unreal Engine. “Even uncertainty about the Unreal Engine’s ability to continue supporting iOS and macOS will make it less likely for Microsoft (and, I believe, other game creators) to select Unreal Engine for their projects,” he wrote. “When game creators are planning development projects, which can last for years, it is important to have confidence that the chosen engine will continue to be available on and support all platforms on which the game creators plan to distribute their games.”
 Unreal Engine is primarily used for the development of console and PC games, but it has a growing mobile business that’s become increasingly important as the game industry has shifted toward cross-platform play. Epic specifically touts a developer toolkit that works in tandem with Unreal Engine, called  Epic Online Services, that allows games to take advantage of the technology developed initially for  Fortnite. The engine’s cross-platform nature is a selling point that makes it one of the go-to platforms for building virtual worlds. Without an Apple developer account, Epic can’t promise developers interested in iOS any of those benefits, an unwelcome shock as its main competitor  Unity prepares to go public.
 In Monday’s hearing, an Epic lawyer argued that even just the threat of Apple terminating the account associated with the Unreal Engine has sent developers “fleeing” from the platform. “So far as Epic is concerned, the Unreal Engine will be actually destroyed. It will no longer be a usable engine,” argued Katherine Forrest of the law firm Cravath, Swaine & Moore, which is representing Epic in the case. “If Epic cannot offer that with the Unreal Engine, Unreal Engine will cease to exist. We’re receiving information from developers saying they’re fleeing the Unreal Engine now.”
 For companies that are also building smaller, less graphics-intensive projects — like indie games — that can be ported to multiple platforms, the lack of iOS support for Unreal Engine would be a deal-breaker. For a game developer, switching to another engine often means scrapping almost every hour of work on an ongoing project and starting entirely from scratch.
 The Unreal Engine is also increasingly important for Hollywood projects using real-time rendering. In particular, Disney and Lucasfilm have turned to Epic’s engine as a tool for filmmaking, using it to create virtual sets, characters, and animations for large  Star Wars projects, including  Solo,  Rogue One, and  The Mandalorian.
  More than half of the first season of  The Mandalorian was in fact shot using custom LED walls that displayed virtual sets, all  powered by the Unreal Engine to allow the crew to see in real time what the shot was going to look like weeks and months before post-production and special effects teams finished the final product. Disney and Lucasfilm did not respond to a request for comment on how developing  Star Wars projects may be affected by the ongoing litigation.
 Unreal Engine is also used by automakers like Audi, Porsche, and Volkswagen to help design cars using visualization and other software processes. Architectural firms use the engine for similar reasons, allowing those companies to perform real-time visualizations of in-progress projects. Epic also licenses Unreal for broadcasting and live event production, with the engine capable of producing real-time graphics, overlays, and other real-time rendered assets.
 One high-profile example is  The Weather Channel’s now-viral mixed reality segments that blend the effects and visuals of simulated natural disasters around real-world hosts in the studio, all using Unreal Engine.
  Many of those projects don’t require iOS functionality, but they may run their backend systems on macOS, which will be affected in a more limited way. Without macOS support in Unreal Engine, Epic loses access to macOS testing tools and the ability to issue speedy updates that may contain bug fixes and security patches. Apple also now requires  notarization — in other words, Apple approval — of third-party apps even on macOS so long as they have a developer ID. Without that developer ID or notarization, Unreal Engine users would have to effectively seek out updates and click through security warnings Apple imposes upon third-party software it has not reviewed.
 “Imagine losing access to your Google account, and everything that comes with it,” one iOS developer, who wished not to be named for fear of repercussions from speaking negatively about Apple, tells  The Verge. “Apple’s very much moving to a Mac that requires software to be signed and notarized with Apple, and Epic would no longer be able to do that. Right now, trying to run unsigned software on the Mac shows scary warnings and can be sidestepped. In the future, not so certain.”
 “It would be the end of the line for Unreal Engine on the Mac.”
 The developer says, like with iOS, the effects of losing macOS support won’t be felt immediately, but the risk might scare companies away. “Because of that uncertainty, developers would no longer be able to rely on Unreal Engine to support Apple’s platforms. For example, Epic would not be able to release their tools to support Apple Silicon, so effectively it would be the end of the line for Unreal Engine on the Mac,” they said.
 It’s too early to tell whether the rhetoric of Epic’s legal team — and claims the Unreal Engine would be “absolutely destroyed” — is hyperbole, or whether Apple really does have the power to cut off a multibillion-dollar company at the knees by terminating a single developer account or even just threatening to do so. But Epic has committed to continuing its fight without walking back the  Fortnite update that initiated its unprecedented showdown with Apple. And Apple, it’s now clear, is willing to do everything in its power to ensure Epic doesn’t blaze a trail other developers may be keen on following.
title: 看看庞大的虚幻引擎生态系统，从游戏开发商到好莱坞制片厂再到汽车制造商，如果苹果禁止Epici的开发者账户，这些生态系统可能会受到影响
title_en: A look at the vast Unreal Engine ecosystem, from game developers to Hollywood studios to car makers, that may be affected if Apple bans Epic's developer account
